services.name: dotcms
services.description: Hybrid headless content management system for building modern websites, apps and digital experiences with a Java-based, API-first platform.
services.installation:
Default admin login is `admin@dotcms.com` / `admin`. Change DOT_INITIAL_ADMIN_PASSWORD before exposing the instance.

Provisions two addons automatically:
- PostgreSQL 16 via the CloudNativePG operator (`dotcms-db`) â€” dotCMS supports PostgreSQL 13-16. Requires the CloudNativePG operator on the cluster.
- OpenSearch 1.3.x (`dotcms-opensearch`) â€” dotCMS does not support OpenSearch 2.x or 3.x. Do not bump the image tag.

This is the single-instance tier. For a clustered, failover-capable deployment (3-node Postgres + 2 dotCMS replicas) use the dotCMS (HA) template.

First boot takes 2-5 minutes while dotCMS initializes the schema and indices. Wait for both addons to become Ready before the dotCMS pod will pass its startup checks.

services.description: Free and open-source online accounting software for small businesses and freelancers, with invoicing, expense tracking, and reporting.
services.installation: Akaunting is a Laravel app backed by MariaDB/MySQL (it does NOT support Postgres). On first boot the entrypoint installs the schema and seeds the admin user from ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD, then the web installer is bypassed. Rotate the MariaDB password (default 'akaunting'), ADMIN_PASSWORD, and APP_KEY (must be a 32-char value; the placeholder is intentionally weak — regenerate with 'php artisan key:generate' or set base64:<openssl rand -base64 32>). After the DB add-on is healthy, log in at the app URL with ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD and change the admin password immediately. Uploaded files, generated PDFs, and logos persist under /var/www/html/storage (mounted volume).

services.name: attendize
services.description: Open-source ticket selling and event management platform (Laravel) for organizing events and selling tickets online.
services.installation: Attendize auto-configures on first boot — no /install wizard. Log in at {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}/login with admin@example.com / changeme (change immediately). Sample organiser and demo events are pre-loaded. A queue worker runs in the background for PDF tickets and email jobs. SMTP is still placeholder (smtp.example.com) — set MAIL_HOST/MAIL_PORT/MAIL_USERNAME/MAIL_PASSWORD to a real relay for ticket delivery. ROTATE before production: APP_KEY, MariaDB db/user/password (defaults attendize/attendize/attendize). Uses MariaDB (MySQL-only, not Postgres) and Valkey/Redis for queue + cache.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: authelia
services.description: Open-source authentication and authorization server providing SSO and multi-factor auth as a companion to reverse proxies via a login portal.
services.installation: Authelia is an SSO / 2FA portal that protects other apps via your reverse proxy (forward-auth / OpenID Connect); it is not a destination app on its own. It needs a YAML config file that environment variables cannot fully replace - access control rules, the authentication backend (file or LDAP), the session cookie domain, and the SMTP/notifier must be defined in /config/configuration.yml. A persistent volume is mounted at /config to hold that file plus the bundled file-based users database (users_database.yml); seed both on first boot (exec into the pod or pre-populate the volume) or Authelia will exit at startup. Production state is stored in the postgres add-on (set AUTHELIA_STORAGE_POSTGRES_* - rotate the password) and session data in the valkey add-on (Redis-compatible, no password). ROTATE the three secret env values (AUTHELIA_SESSION_SECRET, AUTHELIA_STORAGE_ENCRYPTION_KEY, AUTHELIA_IDENTITY_VALIDATION_RESET_PASSWORD_JWT_SECRET) with 'openssl rand -hex 32' before going live. Email-based flows (password reset, second-factor registration) require an SMTP server configured under notifier in the config file. Authelia listens on HTTP port 9091; TLS is terminated at the Kubero ingress.

services.name: automatisch
services.description: Open-source business automation tool (a self-hostable Zapier alternative) for connecting apps and building no-code integration workflows.
services.installation: Automatisch is a multi-service stack. This template runs the main web/API server from automatischio/automatisch on port 3000. It REQUIRES a companion WORKER service: deploy a second app with the SAME image (automatischio/automatisch), the SAME environment variables PLUS WORKER=true, and the same Postgres + Redis connection settings -- the worker runs the background queues that actually execute your flows, so without it nothing fires. It needs two operator add-ons (Postgres + Valkey/Redis, both provisioned here). ROTATE before exposing: ENCRYPTION_KEY, WEBHOOK_SECRET_KEY, and APP_SECRET_KEY (generate each with `openssl rand -base64 36`) plus the Postgres add-on password. On first start the server runs DB migrations and seeds the admin user; the default login is created from the docker env or the install wizard -- check the docs and change the admin password immediately. Set HOST/PROTOCOL to match your public URL so OAuth redirect URIs and webhook URLs resolve correctly.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: azuracast
services.description: Self-hosted, all-in-one web radio management suite for running internet radio stations, with streaming, automation, and live DJ support.
services.installation: AzuraCast is a multi-container stack; this template runs the PRIMARY web/application image (ghcr.io/azuracast/azuracast) which bundles nginx, PHP-FPM and the supervisord-managed background workers (scheduler, message queue, sync tasks) inside the single image, plus the Liquidsoap/Icecast/SHOUTcast streaming processes. External datastores are wired via add-ons: a MariaDB add-on (AzuraCast supports ONLY MySQL/MariaDB, not Postgres) and a Valkey add-on used as the Redis cache (REDIS_HOST points at the Valkey service; Valkey is Redis-protocol compatible and password-less here). Setting MYSQL_HOST to a non-localhost value automatically disables the image's bundled internal MariaDB; ENABLE_REDIS=true + REDIS_HOST disables the bundled Redis. Rotate MARIADB_PASSWORD / MYSQL_PASSWORD and the MariaDB add-on password before going live. The container's HTTP port is 80 (also exposes 443 for TLS and dynamic broadcast ports 8000-8999 for station streams, which must be exposed separately via a NodePort/LoadBalancer for listeners to connect). On first run, browse to the app URL and complete the web setup wizard to create the super-admin account and your first station. Station media, recordings and config persist on the mounted /var/azuracast volume; for production-scale media libraries consider increasing the volume size or using S3-backed station storage (configured per-station in the AzuraCast UI).
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: bigcapital
services.description: Open-source double-entry accounting and financial reporting platform, a self-hosted alternative to QuickBooks, Xero and Wave.
services.installation: Multi-container stack. This template runs the PRIMARY backend API only: bigcapitalhq/server (listens on :3000). It is NOT usable on its own — you MUST also deploy these companion services and route them behind a reverse proxy (Envoy/Nginx): (1) FRONTEND/webapp: bigcapitalhq/webapp:latest (the SPA UI); (2) GATEWAY/proxy: envoyproxy/envoy:v1.30-latest fronting / -> webapp and /api -> server, terminating TLS on 80/443; (3) PDF rendering: gotenberg/gotenberg:7 (point GOTENBERG_URL at it, e.g. http://gotenberg:9000); (4) DB MIGRATION: a one-shot run of the same bigcapitalhq/server image with the migration command to provision the system + tenant schemas before first boot. The server multi-tenants: SYSTEM_DB_NAME is the control DB and each org gets its own DB prefixed by TENANT_DB_NAME_PERFIX. Add-ons wired: MariaDB (bigcapital-mysql:3306, app uses MySQL—not Postgres) and Valkey/Redis (rfr-bigcapital-valkey-readwrite:6379, no password) used for both cache and the BullMQ job queue. BEFORE exposing publicly ROTATE: JWT_SECRET (openssl rand -hex 32) and the MariaDB password (default 'bigcapital'). Set BASE_URL to your public URL (pre-filled with {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}). After the stack is up, browse to the gateway URL and create the first account (no default account exists); set SIGNUP_DISABLED=true afterward to lock down registration. Optional integrations (Plaid bank sync, Stripe/Lemon Squeezy billing, S3 attachments, SMTP mail) are configured via additional env vars.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: btcpay
services.description: Self-hosted, open-source Bitcoin and Lightning payment processor with no fees, no third party and full store/POS/invoicing tooling.
services.installation: BTCPay Server is a multi-container stack; this template deploys ONLY the primary btcpayserver web app backed by a Postgres add-on. It WILL NOT process payments until you also deploy its mandatory companion services in the same namespace: (1) NBXplorer - the UTXO indexer, image 'nicolasdorier/nbxplorer:latest', point btcpayserver at it via BTCPAY_BTCEXPLORERURL=http://nbxplorer:32838/ (default already set); NBXplorer needs its OWN database (set NBXPLORER_POSTGRES) and must reach a Bitcoin node. (2) A pruned/full Bitcoin Core node, image 'btcpayserver/bitcoin:latest' (txindex+RPC), referenced by NBXplorer's NBXPLORER_BTCRPCURL/BTCRPCAUTH - this requires a large persistent volume (hundreds of GB unfunded if not pruned). (3) OPTIONAL Lightning: an LND node ('btcpayserver/lnd:latest') or Core Lightning ('btcpayserver/lightning:latest') wired with BTCPAY_BTCLIGHTNING. ROTATE before first boot: the Postgres password (default 'btcpay') and the BTCPAY_POSTGRES connection string password to match. BTCPAY_HOST is pre-filled with {{KUBERO_APP_HOST}} and must equal the public hostname (used for cookie/URL generation); the app binds on 0.0.0.0:49392 via BTCPAY_BIND. TLS is terminated at the ingress so BTCPAY_PROTOCOL=https and the reverse-proxy headers are honored. First run: open the app URL, register the first account (it becomes the admin), then create a Store and add the BTC (and optional Lightning) wallet. Persistent data (keys, plugins, logs) lives under /datadir on the mounted volume.

services.name: castopod
services.description: Open-source, self-hosted podcast hosting platform with built-in analytics and ActivityPub federation for the Fediverse (Podcasting 2.0).
services.installation: Castopod is a PHP/CodeIgniter app that needs a MySQL-compatible database (the MariaDB add-on here; Castopod does NOT support Postgres) and optionally Redis for caching, which is wired to the password-less Valkey add-on (CP_CACHE_HANDLER=redis + CP_REDIS_HOST). The public URL is injected via {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} into CP_BASEURL; this MUST match the real host or asset/feed URLs break. ROTATE before going live: CP_ANALYTICS_SALT (set to a fresh `openssl rand -hex 32` value — it salts analytics IP hashing and must not be the placeholder), and the MariaDB add-on credentials (db/user/password all default to 'castopod'). On first run, browse to the app URL to complete the web installer and create the super-admin (instance owner) account. Uploaded episode audio, cover art and the media library persist on the /app/public/media volume; raise its size for large podcast catalogs. Outbound email (SMTP) for notifications/invites must be configured in the admin panel after install. ActivityPub federation works out of the box once CP_BASEURL is reachable over HTTPS.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: chiefonboarding
services.description: Open-source employee onboarding and preboarding platform with to-do items, intro sequences, courses and Slack-driven workflows.
services.installation: Django app served on port 8000. Companion add-on: PostgreSQL (primary datastore via DATABASE_URL). Database migrations and static-file collection run automatically on container boot. Rotate SECRET_KEY to a unique random value (openssl rand -hex 32) and rotate the postgres password before exposing publicly. ALLOWED_HOSTS and BASE_URL are wired to this deployment's public host/URL. On first launch an initial admin account is created automatically: log in with email admin@chiefonboarding.com and password admin, then change those credentials immediately. Email (SMTP) is required to invite new hires and send notifications: set EMAIL_HOST, EMAIL_PORT, EMAIL_HOST_USER, EMAIL_HOST_PASSWORD and DEFAULT_FROM_EMAIL, or use a provider like Mailgun via ANYMAIL_* keys. Optional Slack, Google and Twilio integrations can be configured later from the Settings page.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: civicrm
services.description: Open-source constituent relationship management for nonprofits and civic groups, handling contacts, contributions, events, memberships and email.
services.installation: Runs CiviCRM Standalone (civicrm/civicrm). Requires a MariaDB/MySQL database (the bundled mariadb addon, default db/user/password 'civicrm' — rotate before production). On first run the install script provisions the admin account from CIVICRM_ADMIN_USER / CIVICRM_ADMIN_PASS — rotate the password and rotate the DB password too. CIVICRM_UF_BASEURL is wired to the app URL token. CiviCRM does NOT support PostgreSQL. Persistent paths public/, private/ and ext/ are mounted as volumes; back these up. For outbound email and SMS configure an external SMTP provider from the CiviCRM admin UI after install.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: convex
services.description: Open-source reactive backend-as-a-service with a TypeScript database, server functions, file storage, and real-time sync.
services.installation: This template deploys ONLY the Convex backend (ghcr.io/get-convex/convex-backend). It exposes two HTTP ports: 3210 is the client API / sync endpoint (containerPort, what you publish as CONVEX_CLOUD_ORIGIN) and 3211 serves HTTP actions / the 'site' origin (CONVEX_SITE_ORIGIN). Because Kubero routes a single ingress to one port, the public URL maps to 3210; if you need HTTP actions reachable externally, expose port 3211 with a second route or host and update CONVEX_SITE_ORIGIN accordingly. COMPANION SERVICE: deploy the Convex dashboard separately as its own app from image ghcr.io/get-convex/convex-dashboard:latest (listens on 6791), and point its NEXT_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_URL at this backend's CONVEX_CLOUD_ORIGIN. Database: the bundled Postgres add-on holds all application data; note POSTGRES_URL must be the SERVER connection string WITHOUT a database path (Convex creates and manages its own database named after INSTANCE_NAME). DO_NOT_REQUIRE_SSL=true is set because the in-cluster Postgres uses plaintext; remove it if you front Postgres with TLS. ROTATE before going live: INSTANCE_SECRET (openssl rand -hex 32) and the Postgres password. FIRST RUN: after the backend is up, generate a deploy admin key with `kubectl exec <backend-pod> -- ./generate_admin_key.sh` (or `docker compose exec backend ./generate_admin_key.sh` style) and use it as CONVEX_SELF_HOSTED_ADMIN_KEY when running `npx convex deploy/dev` against CONVEX_SELF_HOSTED_URL (the 3210 origin). File storage defaults to the local persistent volume at /convex/data; for production-scale file/snapshot storage Convex can use S3-compatible object storage via the AWS_* / S3_STORAGE_*_BUCKET env vars (not configured here).

services.name: countly
services.description: Open-source, privacy-focused product analytics platform for tracking web, mobile and desktop apps with funnels, retention, crashes and push.
services.installation: Countly Community Edition is a multi-process stack. This template runs the PRIMARY all-in-one image countly/countly-server, which bundles Nginx plus BOTH Node.js processes (the API ingestion process and the frontend dashboard) behind a single HTTP port 80. DATASTORE: Countly stores everything in MongoDB. There is no native MongoDB add-on, so this template provisions the MongoDB-wire-compatible 'documentdb' add-on (Microsoft DocumentDB, SCRAM-SHA-256 + TLS, user mongoadmin). COMPATIBILITY CAVEAT: Countly targets genuine MongoDB and uses some Mongo-specific server behaviour; DocumentDB covers the common wire protocol but full feature parity is not guaranteed - if you hit issues, point COUNTLY_CONFIG__MONGODB at an external real MongoDB 6.x/7.x instance instead and drop the add-on. The connection is supplied as a full URI via COUNTLY_CONFIG__MONGODB (note the DOUBLE underscores - this is Countly's universal config-override prefix; per-process equivalents are COUNTLY_CONFIG_API__ / COUNTLY_CONFIG_FRONTEND__). ROTATE before production: the documentdb add-on password (default 'countly') and keep the URI in sync. FIRST-RUN: open the site, complete the on-screen setup wizard to create the global admin account, then add your first application to get its App Key/API Key for SDK integration. COMPANION / SCALE-OUT SERVICES (NOT deployed by this single-container template): for horizontal scale Countly splits into separate images - countly/api (API ingestion, listens on port 3001) and countly/frontend (dashboard, listens on port 6001) - each pointed at the same MongoDB via COUNTLY_CONFIG__MONGODB; scale countly/api replicas to match write traffic. The all-in-one image also runs internal cron/aggregation and plugin jobs in-process, so no separate scheduler/worker container is required for a single-node install. The mounted /opt/countly/frontend/express/public/appimages volume keeps uploaded app icons/avatars; plugin and config state otherwise lives in MongoDB.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: crater
services.description: Open-source, self-hosted web and mobile app to track expenses, payments and create professional invoices and estimates.
services.installation: Crater is a Laravel (PHP) + Vue invoicing app backed by MariaDB. This template runs the maintained community image ghcr.io/jee-r/crater (nginx + PHP-FPM on :8080), since the upstream project ships a build-from-source docker-compose rather than an official prebuilt image. BEFORE first boot ROTATE: APP_KEY (run `openssl rand -base64 32` and prefix with `base64:`, i.e. set APP_KEY=base64:<value>) and the MariaDB password (default 'crater'). APP_URL is pre-filled with {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} and MUST match the public URL or cookie-based auth (4.0.0+) will fail. FIRST RUN: browse to the app URL and complete the on-boarding wizard (admin account, company, currency); the wizard runs DB migrations automatically. Persistent app data (uploads, generated PDF invoices, logo, storage/) lives under /app and is kept on the mounted volume. Cache/queue/session default to the database and file drivers (no Redis required); SMTP for mail and optional S3 for storage are configured later via env vars or the Settings UI.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: cryptgeon
services.description: Secure, open-source service for sharing self-destructing, end-to-end-encrypted notes and files, inspired by PrivNote.
services.installation: Deploy with the bundled Valkey (Redis-compatible) add-on, which is where all encrypted notes/files are stored temporarily until they expire or are viewed; nothing is persisted on disk, so no volume is required. The app connects to Valkey via the REDIS env var (passwordless in-cluster). Encryption happens client-side in the browser using AES-GCM, so the server never sees plaintext or keys and there is no admin login or user database to seed. Tune SIZE_LIMIT (max payload, e.g. 4 MiB), MAX_VIEWS and MAX_EXPIRATION (minutes) to taste. Because data lives only in Valkey memory, restarting/redeploying Valkey clears all outstanding notes. No SMTP, S3 or external search engine is needed.

services.name: cryptomator
services.description: Self-hosted key management and access control server for sharing Cryptomator-encrypted vaults across teams.
services.installation: Cryptomator Hub is a Quarkus app that REQUIRES two companion services you must deploy separately: (1) a PostgreSQL database (provided here as the 'postgres' add-on; rotate the default cryptomator/cryptomator/cryptomator db/user/password) and (2) a Keycloak identity server (NOT in the add-on catalog, deploy it separately, e.g. quay.io/keycloak/keycloak, and import the Hub realm). Set HUB_PUBLIC_URL / HUB_KEYCLOAK_PUBLIC_URL to your real ingress URLs and configure the Keycloak realm/clients. The QUARKUS_OIDC_AUTH_SERVER_URL and HUB_KEYCLOAK_* values below point at a Keycloak you must stand up first. Use the official Setup Wizard at cryptomator.org/hub/self-hosted to generate realm config. Rotate all default credentials before exposing publicly.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: dittofeed
services.description: Open-source customer engagement and marketing automation platform for building messaging campaigns and user journeys across email, SMS, and push.
services.installation: Dittofeed is a multi-service stack. This template runs the all-in-one dittofeed/dittofeed-lite image (API + dashboard + worker in one process) on port 3000 in single-tenant mode. It needs two operator add-ons (Postgres + ClickHouse, both provisioned here) AND an external Temporal cluster, which is NOT an available add-on: deploy Temporal separately (image temporalio/auto-setup:1.22.4 with its own Postgres, plus optional temporalio/ui:2.22.1) and point TEMPORAL_ADDRESS at it (default temporal:7233). For production scale you may instead run the split images dittofeed/api, dittofeed/dashboard, and dittofeed/worker (worker handles the Temporal-driven journey processing) rather than the lite bundle; blob storage (any S3/MinIO) is optional. BOOTSTRAP=true auto-creates the default workspace and tables on first start. ROTATE before exposing: SECRET_KEY (openssl rand -base64 32), the admin PASSWORD env, plus the Postgres and ClickHouse add-on passwords. Set DASHBOARD_API_BASE / dashboard URLs to your public host. CLICKHOUSE_HOST must be the full http://host:8123 URL.

services.name: docassemble
services.description: Open-source expert system for guided interviews and document assembly, widely used to build legal-aid and law-firm self-help applications.
services.installation: Docassemble ships as a single all-in-one image (jhpyle/docassemble) whose roles are selected with the CONTAINERROLE env var. This template runs the web role and externalises the SQL database and Redis to the bundled add-ons. ROTATE the Postgres password and SECRETKEY (openssl rand -base64 32) before production. The container listens on internal port 80; set BEHINDHTTPSLOADBALANCER=true because Kubero's ingress terminates TLS, and DAHOSTNAME is auto-set from {{KUBERO_APP_HOST}}. On first boot the schema is created automatically; the bootstrap admin is admin@admin.com / password (set/rotate via the configuration). Persistent application data (uploaded packages, generated files, certs, config.yml) lives under /usr/share/docassemble on the volume. COMPANION SERVICES: for a production multi-server install deploy additional replicas of the SAME jhpyle/docassemble image with different CONTAINERROLE values that share the same Postgres, Redis and /usr/share/docassemble volume: CONTAINERROLE=celery (background task / document-assembly worker), CONTAINERROLE=cron (scheduled tasks), and optionally CONTAINERROLE=log (centralised logging) and CONTAINERROLE=mail. RabbitMQ is only needed for the SQL/Redis-replicated HA topology (set RABBITMQ=pyamqp://...) and can be added later; a single-node web+celery setup does not require it. A background OCR/translation ML container is not required for basic operation. To externalise file storage to S3/Azure instead of the volume, set the S3 or AZURE config keys in the docassemble configuration (not an operator add-on).

services.name: docspell
services.description: Self-hosted document organizer (DMS) that auto-tags, OCRs and full-text-searches your scanned PDFs, emails and uploads.
services.installation: This is the Docspell rest server (web UI + REST API) listening on internal port 7880. It REQUIRES a companion job executor deployed alongside it for OCR, text extraction and document conversion: deploy docspell/joex:latest pointed at the SAME Postgres add-on (set its DOCSPELL_JOEX_JDBC_* / DOCSPELL_JOEX_BASE__URL env). Both containers must share the file storage; this template stores binaries in the bundled Postgres (DOCSPELL_SERVER_BACKEND_FILES_DEFAULT__STORE=database), so no shared volume between server and joex is needed for files. Rotate the random secrets before production: DOCSPELL_SERVER_AUTH_SERVER__SECRET (openssl rand -hex 32) and DOCSPELL_SERVER_ADMIN__ENDPOINT_SECRET, and rotate the Postgres password. Full-text search defaults here to the built-in PostgreSQL FTS backend (DOCSPELL_SERVER_FULL__TEXT__SEARCH_ENABLED=true + DOCSPELL_SERVER_FULL__TEXT__SEARCH_BACKEND=postgresql), so no external Apache Solr is needed; only add a Solr instance if you set the backend to solr. First run: open the web UI, register a new account using the same name for both 'collective' and 'user', then log in. Optional companions for inbound mail/scanner ingest (docspell-consumedir, IMAP poller) are described in the docs and reuse the same Postgres.

services.name: documize
services.description: Open-source modern document and knowledge management platform combining wiki, docs and structured templates for teams.
services.installation: Uses the official documize/community image on internal port 5001. Deploy with the bundled PostgreSQL add-on; the DOCUMIZEDB connection string and DOCUMIZEDBTYPE=postgresql point Documize at the 'documize-postgresql-rw' Postgres service (rotate the Postgres add-on password and update DOCUMIZEDB after install). DOCUMIZELOCATION is set to 'selfhost' for self-hosted operation. IMPORTANT: replace DOCUMIZESALT with a unique random string (e.g. openssl rand -hex 16) before going to production. Documize stores all documents, settings and uploads in the database, so no persistent volume is required. On first boot open the app URL to run the setup wizard: name your organization and create the initial admin account; the database schema is migrated automatically on startup.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: drawio
services.description: Self-hosted draw.io (diagrams.net), a client-side editor for flowcharts, UML, ER, network and other diagrams.
services.installation: draw.io is a stateless, client-side diagram editor served by Tomcat. Diagrams are never stored on the server: they live in the browser, on the user's local disk, or in an external storage provider the user connects (Google Drive, OneDrive, GitHub, GitLab, Dropbox), so no database add-on and no persistent volume are required. To enable those external storage integrations you must register OAuth apps with each provider and pass their client IDs via the DRAWIO_GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID / DRAWIO_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID / DRAWIO_GITLAB_CLIENT_ID / DRAWIO_ONEDRIVE_CLIENT_ID env vars (left blank by default). PUBLIC_DNS / DRAWIO_BASE_URL are pre-set to the app URL so generated share/embed links are correct; update them if you change the hostname. For PNG/SVG/PDF server-side export, deploy the optional jgraph/export-server companion container and point DRAWIO_EXPORT_URL at its in-cluster service name (export then works client-side without it). No SMTP, S3 or search engine is needed.

services.name: dremio
services.description: Open lakehouse platform with a SQL query engine and self-service semantic layer over data lakes, databases, and object storage.
services.installation: This template deploys a SINGLE-NODE Dremio Community Edition (dremio-oss) running coordinator + executor in one pod, which is the supported way to self-host/evaluate the OSS engine. Dremio OSS is self-contained: it stores its metadata KV store (RocksDB), uploads, and accelerator/cache files on LOCAL DISK under /opt/dremio/data, so NO database add-on is provisioned -- a persistent volume is mounted there instead and MUST remain persistent or you lose all sources, spaces, and reflections. FIRST RUN: open the web UI on port 9047, the very first form creates the admin account and password -- set a strong password there (nothing to rotate in this template). PORTS: 9047 = HTTP web UI / REST API (the containerPort published here); the engine also listens on 31010 (Dremio ODBC/native client + coordinator-executor RPC), 32010 (Apache Arrow Flight, used by Python/JDBC Arrow clients), and 45678 (inter-node coordination) -- expose those via additional Services if external clients/BI tools need them. SCALING: to run a real distributed cluster you deploy SEPARATE COMPANION apps from the SAME image `dremio/dremio-oss` configured as dedicated roles -- (1) a coordinator-only node (DREMIO_JAVA_SERVER_EXTRA_OPTS with services.coordinator.enabled=true, services.executor.enabled=false), (2) one or more executor-only nodes (services.coordinator.enabled=false, services.executor.enabled=true) that register back to the coordinator over 31010/45678, and (3) an external distributed store (S3/HDFS) for the dist path instead of the local PVC; these companions are NOT created here. NOTE: Dremio OSS uses external object stores (S3/ADLS/GCS/HDFS) and JDBC sources for actual data -- those are configured at runtime in the UI, not as add-ons. Bump the app pod size (Dremio is a JVM and needs several GiB of heap) before loading real workloads.

services.name: drupal
services.description: Drupal is an open-source content management framework for building websites, intranets and digital experiences.
services.installation: The official drupal image installs interactively via the web setup wizard on first run; complete the wizard at the app URL. On the database step choose PostgreSQL and enter host '{{KUBERO_APP_NAME}}-postgresql-rw', port 5432, database/user/password 'drupal' (rotate the postgres add-on credentials and the wizard admin password after setup). Drupal writes settings.php and uploaded files under /var/www/html/sites, which is persisted by the drupal-sites volume. For production, increase PHP memory_limit and the app pod size for large sites. No external object store or search engine is required for a base install (add Solr/Search API later if needed).

services.name: element
services.description: Glossy, feature-rich Matrix web client for secure, decentralized chat, voice and video collaboration.
services.installation: Element Web is a STATELESS static client (nginx on port 80); it does NOT store data itself. It is the frontend for a Matrix homeserver, which you must deploy separately. Run a Synapse homeserver (which needs its own Postgres) and point Element at it. Configure the homeserver/identity-server URLs by mounting a config.json at /app/config.json or via the ELEMENT_WEB_PORT / templated config; at minimum set default_server_config (m.homeserver base_url) to your Synapse URL. No add-ons or volumes are required for Element Web itself.

services.name: emqx
services.description: Massively scalable open-source MQTT broker for IoT, connecting millions of devices with a built-in dashboard, rules engine and data bridges.
services.installation: EMQX is self-contained: it stores cluster state, sessions, retained messages and config in its built-in Mnesia/RLOG database persisted to the emqx-data volume at /opt/emqx/data, so no external add-on is provisioned. The HTTP management dashboard and REST API listen on port 18083; the MQTT listeners (1883 TCP, 8883 TLS, 8083 WS, 8084 WSS) are exposed by the image but are not the HTTP container port and need a separate Service/Ingress if you want external MQTT access. On first run log in to the dashboard with the default account admin / public and change the password immediately (EMQX_DASHBOARD__DEFAULT_PASSWORD is set to a placeholder here that you should rotate, or just change it in the UI). EMQX_NODE__COOKIE is the Erlang distribution secret shared by cluster members and MUST be rotated from the template default before production. Single-node runs on the open-source edition; from v5.9 clustering requires a license key from EMQX, and optional auth/data integrations (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, Kafka, MongoDB, etc.) are configured later in the dashboard, not at boot.

services.name: eneo
services.description: Open-source, multi-tenant AI platform for the public sector: build chat assistants, RAG knowledge bases and AI workflows on your own models.
services.installation:
PRIMARY container = the FastAPI backend (ghcr.io/eneo-ai/eneo-backend, listens on :8000, served behind a reverse proxy). This template deploys ONLY the backend API. To run the full stack you must also deploy these companion services as separate apps, all sharing this app's env (especially the POSTGRES_*, REDIS_*, JWT_SECRET, URL_SIGNING_KEY, ENCRYPTION_KEY values):
  - Frontend (SvelteKit UI, the user-facing site on :3000): image ghcr.io/eneo-ai/eneo-frontend:latest. Point its API base at this backend.
  - Worker (ARQ background tasks: web crawls, uploads, integrations): SAME image ghcr.io/eneo-ai/eneo-backend:latest with env RUN_AS_WORKER=true.
  - DB init (one-shot migrations + default tenant/user; run ONCE then it exits): SAME backend image with command ["python","init_db.py"]. Run this before the backend/worker the first time, or run `python init_db.py` in the backend container.
DATASTORES: Postgres MUST have the pgvector extension (the upstream compose uses pgvector/pgvector:pg16) — Eneo stores embeddings in pgvector; confirm the operator-provisioned Postgres has the `vector` extension available/enabled. Redis is provided here by the Valkey add-on (used by ARQ for the job queue).
SECRETS TO ROTATE before production: JWT_SECRET, URL_SIGNING_KEY, ENCRYPTION_KEY (generate each with `openssl rand -hex 32`), and the Postgres password. Also set PUBLIC_ORIGIN to your real https URL (pre-filled with this app's URL token).
LLM PROVIDERS: Eneo brings its own models — set at least one of OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY / GEMINI_API_KEY / AZURE_* / MISTRAL_API_KEY, or point VLLM_MODEL_URL at a self-hosted model. Optional: INFINITY_URL for embeddings, TAVILY_API_KEY for web search.
FIRST RUN: after db-init, log in with the default admin user created by init_db.py and immediately change its password.

services.name: erpnext
services.description: Open-source ERP suite (accounting, inventory, HR, CRM, manufacturing, projects) built on the Frappe framework.
services.installation: ERPNext is a MULTI-CONTAINER Frappe stack. This template deploys the PRIMARY backend (gunicorn) image frappe/erpnext, which serves the Frappe HTTP API/app on port 8000. To run a complete, production-ready install you MUST deploy these companion services from the SAME frappe/erpnext image (only the command differs) plus the nginx frontend, and run one-time site creation: (1) frontend - image frappe/erpnext, command 'nginx-entrypoint.sh', env BACKEND=<this-app>:8000, SOCKETIO=<websocket>:9000, FRAPPE_SITE_NAME_HEADER, UPSTREAM_REAL_IP_ADDRESS - this is the public web server (port 8080). (2) websocket - image frappe/erpnext, command 'node /home/frappe/frappe-bench/apps/frappe/socketio.js' (port 9000, real-time). (3) queue-short - command 'bench worker --queue short,default'. (4) queue-long - command 'bench worker --queue long,default,short'. (5) scheduler - command 'bench schedule'. (6) configurator (run-once init) - command writes common_site_config.json from DB_HOST/DB_PORT/REDIS_CACHE/REDIS_QUEUE/SOCKETIO_PORT then exits. (7) create-site (run-once Job) - 'bench new-site' with --db-type mariadb, --mariadb-root-password, --admin-password, then 'bench --site <site> install-app erpnext' and 'bench use <site>'. All services share the /home/frappe/frappe-bench/sites volume (the extraVolume below). FIRST RUN: rotate the MariaDB password (default 'erpnext'), set a strong Administrator password during create-site, then log in at the app URL as user 'Administrator'. SEARCH (optional): full-text search uses the built-in DB by default; ERPNext can optionally integrate an external search backend - omitted here. Frappe officially supports MariaDB (first-class) and PostgreSQL; this template uses the MariaDB add-on as the canonical/recommended database.

services.name: erugo
services.description: Self-hosted, WeTransfer-style file-sharing platform with secure links, passwords, and download limits, built on Laravel and Vue.
services.installation: Erugo stores all data (SQLite database, uploaded files, and config) under /var/www/html/storage, which is backed by a persistent volume; no external database add-on is required. The app listens on port 80. On first run, open the app URL to complete the setup wizard and create the initial admin account. Erugo generates its own APP_KEY on first boot if one is not provided. The container expects exclusive control of the mounted storage directory.

services.name: espocrm
services.description: Open-source CRM for sales and customer management: contacts, accounts, leads, opportunities, activities, workflows, reporting and email.
services.installation: Runs the official EspoCRM image (espocrm/espocrm, apache variant). EspoCRM only supports MySQL/MariaDB (no PostgreSQL) — this template ships the bundled mariadb addon (default db/user/password 'espocrm', rotate before production and keep ESPOCRM_DATABASE_PASSWORD in sync). On first boot the admin account is provisioned from ESPOCRM_ADMIN_USERNAME / ESPOCRM_ADMIN_PASSWORD — rotate ESPOCRM_ADMIN_PASSWORD (placeholder value). ESPOCRM_SITE_URL is wired to the app URL token so generated links resolve. The valkey addon is wired as an optional Redis cache backend (Valkey is Redis-compatible and password-less here) via ESPOCRM_CONFIG_USE_CACHE / the Redis host/port vars; remove it if not wanted. Persistent app data (uploads, custom code, config, extensions) lives at /var/www/html — back this up. EspoCRM needs a scheduled cron job hitting cron.php every ~1 minute; run it via an external CronJob after install. For outbound email configure SMTP from the EspoCRM admin UI.

services.name: flarum
services.description: Flarum is a simple, fast, free and open-source forum software for building modern community discussion platforms.
services.installation: Uses the community-maintained mondedie/flarum image (the official flarum/flarum repo ships source, not a runnable image). On first boot the container runs the installer non-interactively from the env vars below against the MariaDB add-on, creating the admin account from FLARUM_ADMIN_USER / FLARUM_ADMIN_PASS (min 8 chars) / FLARUM_ADMIN_MAIL. ROTATE the admin password, the mariadb add-on credentials (db/user/password all default to 'flarum'), and log in to change them after setup. FORUM_URL is wired to the live app URL via {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}. Persistent data (installed extensions, compiled public assets, logs) lives under /flarum/app and is kept by the flarum-data volume; for production raise the PHP memory limit and pod size if you install many extensions.

services.name: flatnotes
services.description: Self-hosted, database-less note-taking web app that stores everything as a flat folder of portable Markdown files with full-text search.
services.installation: Flatnotes is database-less: all notes are stored as plain Markdown files under /data (mounted on the flatnotes-data volume), with a Whoosh search index rebuilt automatically on startup. No add-on (Postgres/Redis/etc.) is required. Before deploying, rotate FLATNOTES_PASSWORD and FLATNOTES_SECRET_KEY from the template defaults: set FLATNOTES_SECRET_KEY to a long random string (e.g. `openssl rand -hex 32`) so that auth session tokens stay valid and secure, and FLATNOTES_USERNAME/FLATNOTES_PASSWORD to your own credentials. FLATNOTES_AUTH_TYPE defaults to 'password' (single-user login); set it to 'totp' to add a TOTP second factor (also set FLATNOTES_TOTP_KEY), 'read_only' for a public read-only instance, or 'none' to disable auth entirely (only safe behind another auth proxy). The app listens on port 8080 and serves both the web UI and API. Because notes are just files on the volume, you can back them up, sync them, or edit them with any external Markdown editor. No SMTP, object store or external search engine is needed.

services.name: frappehr
services.description: Open-source HR and payroll suite (employees, leave, attendance, expenses, payroll) built on the Frappe framework and ERPNext.
services.installation: Frappe HR (HRMS) is a MULTI-CONTAINER Frappe stack and has NO standalone official image: it is installed as an app on top of the frappe/erpnext base image (HRMS depends on ERPNext). This template deploys the PRIMARY backend (gunicorn) image frappe/erpnext, which serves the Frappe HTTP API/app on port 8000. To run a complete, production-ready install you MUST deploy these companion services from the SAME frappe/erpnext image (only the command differs) plus the nginx frontend, and run one-time site creation: (1) frontend - image frappe/erpnext, command 'nginx-entrypoint.sh', env BACKEND=<this-app>:8000, SOCKETIO=<websocket>:9000, FRAPPE_SITE_NAME_HEADER, UPSTREAM_REAL_IP_ADDRESS - this is the public web server (port 8080). (2) websocket - image frappe/erpnext, command 'node /home/frappe/frappe-bench/apps/frappe/socketio.js' (port 9000, real-time). (3) queue-short - command 'bench worker --queue short,default'. (4) queue-long - command 'bench worker --queue long,default,short'. (5) scheduler - command 'bench schedule'. (6) configurator (run-once init) - command writes common_site_config.json from DB_HOST/DB_PORT/REDIS_CACHE/REDIS_QUEUE/SOCKETIO_PORT then exits. (7) create-site (run-once Job) - 'bench new-site' with --db-type mariadb, --mariadb-root-password, --admin-password, then 'bench --site <site> install-app erpnext && bench --site <site> install-app hrms', then 'bench use <site>'. The hrms app is what turns this into Frappe HR. All services share the /home/frappe/frappe-bench/sites volume (the extraVolume below). FIRST RUN: rotate the MariaDB password (default 'frappehr'), set a strong Administrator password during create-site, then log in at the app URL as user 'Administrator' and open the HR workspace. Frappe officially supports MariaDB (first-class) and PostgreSQL; this template uses the MariaDB add-on as the canonical/recommended database.

services.name: freescout
services.description: Free self-hosted help desk and shared inbox, a privacy-friendly open-source alternative to Zendesk and Help Scout.
services.installation: Image: nfrastack/freescout (community-maintained, formerly tiredofit). Rotate APP_KEY (base64 of 32 random bytes: `base64:$(openssl rand -base64 32)`), the Postgres password (default 'freescout'), and ADMIN_PASS before exposing publicly. First run auto-installs and seeds the admin user from ADMIN_EMAIL / ADMIN_PASS; change the admin password after first login. DB_TYPE=pgsql points FreeScout at the bundled postgres add-on (host freescout-postgresql-rw). For inbound/outbound email, configure SMTP/IMAP in the FreeScout settings UI (or via mail env vars) after install. Persistent /data volume holds uploads, installed modules, cache and config.

services.name: fugu
services.description: Free, privacy-friendly, simple, open-source and self-hostable product analytics.

ROTATE SECRET_KEY_BASE (a 128-char hex/alphanumeric string; generate with `openssl rand -hex 64`) and rotate the postgres credentials. DATABASE_URL points at the bundled postgres add-on. Set INIT_USER_EMAIL and INIT_USER_PASSWORD to auto-create the first admin on first boot, or visit the root URL and register (ALLOW_REGISTRATION=true). FORCE_SSL_PROD is enabled; terminate TLS at the ingress. App listens on port 3000.

services.name: funkwhale
services.description: Free, federated (ActivityPub) music server and sharing platform - a self-hosted alternative to streaming services for the Fediverse.
services.installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Funkwhale API server container ONLY (funkwhale/api - a Django/gunicorn app listening on HTTP port 5000 via FUNKWHALE_API_IP/FUNKWHALE_API_PORT). Funkwhale is a MULTI-CONTAINER stack and is NOT complete with this pod alone. REQUIRED before first boot: 1) Rotate DJANGO_SECRET_KEY - generate with 'openssl rand -base64 45' (or 'openssl rand -hex 32') and set it; the placeholder MUST be replaced. 2) After the Postgres add-on is up, exec into the api pod and run 'python manage.py migrate' (then 'python manage.py collectstatic --noinput'). 3) Create the first admin with 'python manage.py createsuperuser' (or 'python manage.py fw users create --superuser'). MANDATORY COMPANION SERVICES to deploy as SEPARATE apps (same FUNKWHALE_VERSION, sharing the SAME Postgres + Valkey + media volume): (a) funkwhale/api with command ['celery','-A','funkwhale_api.taskapp','worker','-l','INFO'] - the CELERY WORKER that handles federation delivery, audio import/transcoding, and async tasks (MANDATORY); (b) funkwhale/api with command ['celery','-A','funkwhale_api.taskapp','beat','-l','INFO'] - the CELERY BEAT SCHEDULER for periodic federation/cleanup jobs; (c) funkwhale/front - the NGINX FRONTEND/REVERSE-PROXY image (port 80) that serves the Vue.js SPA, static/media files, and proxies /api to this api pod. Point the funkwhale/front container's FUNKWHALE_API_HOST/FUNKWHALE_API_PORT at this api Service, mount the SAME /srv/funkwhale/data/media (RWX) volume, and route public ingress to funkwhale/front (NOT directly to the api pod). The worker/beat/front pods must share this api pod's media volume - use RWX storage so imported/federated audio is reachable by all. OPTIONAL: a Typesense container (image typesense/typesense) enables full-text search - set TYPESENSE_API_KEY + TYPESENSE_HOST and run 'python manage.py search build'; omit for a basic start. OBJECT STORAGE: for production at scale set AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID / AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY / AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET_NAME / AWS_S3_ENDPOINT_URL at an external S3 bucket (e.g. the RustFS add-on) instead of the local media volume. Rotate the Postgres password; Valkey is password-less in-cluster. FUNKWHALE_HOSTNAME is baked into ActivityPub actor URLs and cannot be changed cleanly after federating, so set it to your real host before going live.

services.name: garage
services.description: Lightweight, self-hosted, S3-compatible distributed object storage built for geo-redundant clusters on commodity hardware.
services.installation: Garage is a single self-contained Rust daemon that keeps all metadata and object data on local disk, so it needs NO database add-on, just the two persistent volumes mounted below (/var/lib/garage/meta and /var/lib/garage/data). The S3 API is served on the container HTTP port 3900; the static web endpoint is 3902 and the admin API is 3903. Garage requires a few settings (rpc_secret, replication factor, bind addresses, dirs) that are normally supplied via /etc/garage.toml; the GARAGE_* env vars below cover the env-config path, but you should mount a garage.toml with replication_factor=1 for a single-node deploy. CRITICAL: rotate GARAGE_RPC_SECRET (a 32-byte hex value, generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`) and GARAGE_ADMIN_TOKEN / GARAGE_METRICS_TOKEN before exposing. First-run setup is CLI-driven: exec into the pod and run `garage layout assign -z dc1 -c 10G <node_id>` then `garage layout apply --version 1`, then create a key (`garage key create`) and a bucket (`garage bucket create`) and grant access. Point S3 clients at the app URL with path-style addressing. Garage is the object store itself, so it has no companion services.

services.name: ghostfolio
services.description: Open-source wealth management software to track stocks, ETFs, crypto and other assets across accounts in one privacy-focused dashboard.

Database migrations run automatically on boot. Rotate ACCESS_TOKEN_SALT and JWT_SECRET_KEY to unique random values (openssl rand -hex 32 each) and rotate the postgres password before exposing publicly. The Valkey add-on runs without a password, so REDIS_PASSWORD is left empty. After first launch, create your account from the start page; the FIRST registered user (or one whose security token you promote) can be granted admin by setting their role to ADMIN in the Postgres _user table. Stock/crypto market data is fetched from external providers (Yahoo Finance by default); configure additional DATA_SOURCE provider API keys via env if you need them.

services.name: glean
services.description: Self-hosted RSS reader and personal knowledge management tool with AI-powered semantic search over your saved reading.
services.installation: This template deploys the PRIMARY backend API only (ghcr.io/leslieleung/glean-backend, FastAPI, port 8000). Glean is a multi-container stack: also deploy the WORKER (same image ghcr.io/leslieleung/glean-backend run as the arq background task queue), the WEB frontend (ghcr.io/leslieleung/glean-web, listens on :80), and the ADMIN dashboard (ghcr.io/leslieleung/glean-admin, listens on :80, default ADMIN_PORT 3001). It also requires a MILVUS vector database (milvusdb/milvus:v2.6.6) plus its etcd (quay.io/coreos/etcd:v3.5.5) and minio (minio/minio) dependencies for semantic search embeddings - Milvus is not an available operator add-on, so deploy it separately and point MILVUS_HOST/MILVUS_PORT at it (default 19530). Postgres and Redis (Valkey) are provisioned by the add-ons below. ROTATE before exposing: SECRET_KEY (JWT signing - replace with `openssl rand -hex 32`), the postgres password, and ADMIN_PASSWORD. First run: CREATE_ADMIN=true auto-creates the super_admin from ADMIN_USERNAME/ADMIN_PASSWORD; RUN_MIGRATIONS=true applies DB migrations on boot. Note: upstream still labels this project as in-development / not production-ready.

services.name: grist
services.description: Open-source modern relational spreadsheet that combines the flexibility of a spreadsheet with the robustness of a database.
services.installation: Grist stores each document as a SQLite .grist file on disk under /persist/docs, so the persistent volume is mandatory and holds all your user data. The bundled PostgreSQL add-on is used only for the Grist 'home' database (orgs, workspaces, users, sharing/ACL metadata) via the TYPEORM_* variables; rotate the Postgres password. Rotate GRIST_SESSION_SECRET to a fresh random value (openssl rand -hex 32) before going live, as it signs session cookies. APP_HOME_URL is wired to the public URL token; keep it correct or document sharing links and the API will break. By default this image runs with anonymous/no-login access (GRIST_DEFAULT_EMAIL is set as the initial admin) which is fine for a trusted single-tenant install; to require login configure an external auth provider (GRIST_FORCE_LOGIN plus SAML/OIDC env vars) or front it with an authenticating proxy. Sandboxing uses gVisor by default and falls back gracefully; no extra service required. No external object store is needed for a single-node deployment.

services.name: hermes
services.description: Open-source document management system from HashiCorp Labs for scaling an organization's writing, review and approval workflows.
services.installation: Hermes is an alpha-stage HashiCorp Labs project that does NOT publish a prebuilt container image; you must build it from the repo (`docker build -t ghcr.io/hashicorp-forge/hermes:latest .`) and push it to a registry you control before deploying this template (update image.repository/tag to match). Hermes is config-driven via an HCL file: mount your `config.hcl` at the path referenced by the entrypoint (this template mounts a volume at /hermes/config; pass `-config=/hermes/config/config.hcl`) or override the database via the HERMES_SERVER_POSTGRES_* env vars set here. A Postgres add-on is provisioned and is the v2 source of truth - rotate the default `hermes` password. REQUIRED external companions NOT provided by this template: (1) Algolia (App ID + Search API Key + Write API Key) for the search index; (2) a Google Workspace project OR SharePoint/Microsoft 365 tenant as the document storage backend AND OAuth identity provider - supply the corresponding OAuth client credentials in config.hcl. Optional: Jira for issue linking. After first boot, run the included DB migrations and index sync per the repo README, then sign in with your Google/Microsoft account to bootstrap the first user.

services.name: hi-events
services.description: Open-source event management and ticket-selling platform (Laravel + React) for creating events, selling tickets, and managing attendees.
services.installation: Hi.Events is a Laravel API + React frontend stack. This template deploys the ALL-IN-ONE image (daveearley/hi.events-all-in-one, container port 80) which bundles the API, the built frontend, nginx, and the queue worker + scheduler in one container, so for a basic deployment NO companion services are required. It is wired to the Postgres and Valkey (Redis) add-ons provisioned here. For a horizontally-scaled production setup you can instead split into separate images, all sharing the SAME DATABASE_URL, Redis and APP_KEY/JWT_SECRET: (1) Backend API - image daveearley/hi.events-backend on port 8000; (2) a queue/scheduler worker from the same backend image with command 'php artisan queue:work' plus a scheduler ('php artisan schedule:work'); (3) Frontend - image daveearley/hi.events-frontend on port 3000, set its VITE_API_URL_CLIENT/VITE_API_URL_SERVER to the backend URL. ROTATE before exposing publicly: APP_KEY (a 32-char value prefixed with 'base64:', e.g. `echo "base64:$(openssl rand -base64 32)"`), JWT_SECRET (`openssl rand -hex 32`), and the Postgres add-on password. SMTP is required to deliver tickets and confirmation emails: set MAIL_HOST/MAIL_PORT/MAIL_USERNAME/MAIL_PASSWORD to a real relay. Payments use Stripe: set STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY / STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET and VITE_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY. The public URLs (APP_FRONTEND_URL / VITE_FRONTEND_URL / APP_URL) are wired to this app's public URL. First run: the container runs migrations on boot; then browse to the site and register the first organiser account (set APP_DISABLE_REGISTRATION=true afterwards to close signups). Ticket images/logos and other uploads are persisted on the mounted volume.
