services.name: hop
services.description: Open-source data orchestration and integration platform (ETL) for designing and running visual data pipelines and workflows in the browser.
services.installation: Deploys the official apache/hop-web image, with the visual designer reachable at the /ui path on the app URL. Apache Hop stores its projects, environments, and metadata as files, so no database add-on is provisioned; the persistent /config volume holds your project and config directory and must be kept. Mount or import your Hop project under /config and set HOP_PROJECT_FOLDER / HOP_PROJECT_NAME / HOP_ENVIRONMENT_NAME to auto-load it on startup (leave them empty to start with the default 'samples' project). Database, file, and message-queue connections used by your pipelines (Postgres, MySQL, Kafka, S3, etc.) are configured inside Hop per pipeline and are not part of this template. For production, run with a sized app pod (the JVM needs more than the default 512Mi) and add authentication in front of Hop Web, which ships without built-in login.

services.name: hortusfox
services.description: Self-hosted collaborative plant management system for tracking, organizing and caring for your indoor and outdoor plants.
services.installation: Image: ghcr.io/danielbrendel/hortusfox-web (official). Uses MariaDB (the mariadb add-on, host hortusfox-mysql). Rotate the MariaDB password (default 'hortusfox') and set a strong APP_ADMIN_PASSWORD (default placeholder) before exposing publicly; the first-run admin account is seeded from APP_ADMIN_EMAIL / APP_ADMIN_PASSWORD on initial migration. DB_CHARSET must be utf8mb4. Persistent volumes hold uploaded plant images (/var/www/html/public/img), backups (/var/www/html/public/backup), custom themes (/var/www/html/public/themes), app logs (/var/www/html/app/logs) and applied migration markers (/var/www/html/app/migrations) so data and migration state survive restarts. Set APP_TIMEZONE to your timezone.

services.name: hyperswitch
services.description: Open-source, Rust-based payment switch and orchestration platform for routing, retrying and reconciling payments across 100+ processors.
services.installation: Hyperswitch is a multi-container stack. This template deploys the PRIMARY API server, the `hyperswitch-router` (the `standalone` image is the all-in-one self-host variant that bundles config + migrations). It listens on HTTP 8080 (health check: GET /health). Add-ons: a Postgres add-on (the system of record) and a Valkey/Redis add-on (used for caching, locking and as the scheduler/queue backend) are provisioned. CONFIG: Hyperswitch is normally driven by a TOML file, but every value can be overridden with `ROUTER__<SECTION>__<KEY>` env vars (double underscores) - that is what this template uses to wire the add-ons. The router reads both a MASTER and a REPLICA database config; both are pointed at the same Postgres add-on here. COMPANION SERVICES to deploy as their own Kubero apps, each with the SAME database + redis env as this router: (1) SCHEDULER PRODUCER -> image `juspaydotin/hyperswitch-producer:standalone` (enqueues scheduled tasks like retries/webhooks); (2) SCHEDULER CONSUMER -> image `juspaydotin/hyperswitch-consumer:standalone` (executes those tasks); both are required for retries, payment-method-tokenization expiry and outgoing webhooks to work. (3) CONTROL CENTER (admin dashboard) -> image `juspaydotin/hyperswitch-control-center:latest` (HTTP 9000); set its api_url to this router's {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}. (4) CHECKOUT/SDK WEB -> image `juspaydotin/hyperswitch-web:latest` (the unified client-side payment SDK, served on 9050/9060). FIRST RUN: the standalone router auto-applies DB migrations on boot; create your first merchant/admin-api-key via the /user/signup or admin APIs. SECRETS to ROTATE before going live: replace the Postgres password and set strong values for `ROUTER__SECRETS__ADMIN_API_KEY`, `ROUTER__SECRETS__MASTER_ENC_KEY` (a 32-byte hex key, `openssl rand -hex 32`) and `ROUTER__SECRETS__JWT_SECRET`. KMS/managed-encryption and an external message queue are optional and NOT provisioned here.

services.name: inventree
services.description: Open-source inventory management and stock-control system for parts, suppliers, builds and orders, with a REST API and barcode support.
services.installation: InvenTree is a multi-container Django stack. This template runs the PRIMARY web/server image 'inventree/inventree:stable' (gunicorn on :8000), wired to Postgres and Valkey (Redis cache). You MUST also deploy these companion services from the SAME 'inventree/inventree:stable' image: (1) WORKER - the django-q background task processor, started with command ['invoke','worker'] (or the bundled 'worker' entrypoint); it shares the same env vars and data volume. (2) PROXY - a Caddy reverse proxy/static-file server (image 'caddy:2-alpine') that fronts the server and serves /static and /media from the shared data volume; in Kubero the ingress already handles TLS, so the Caddy proxy is optional if you point ingress at this app directly, but static/media files must still be served. The server entrypoint auto-runs DB migrations and collectstatic on boot. ROTATE before going live: INVENTREE_SECRET_KEY (run 'openssl rand -hex 32') and the Postgres password. After first boot, create the admin/superuser via 'invoke superuser' in the server pod (or set INVENTREE_ADMIN_USER / INVENTREE_ADMIN_PASSWORD / INVENTREE_ADMIN_EMAIL env vars for auto-creation), then log in at the URL. INVENTREE_SITE_URL is set to the public URL; keep INVENTREE_ALLOWED_HOSTS / INVENTREE_TRUSTED_ORIGINS in sync if you change the hostname. Email (INVENTREE_EMAIL_*) is optional but needed for notifications and password resets.

services.name: invoiceninja
services.description: Open-source invoicing, billing, payments and expense-tracking platform for freelancers and businesses.
services.installation: Invoice Ninja v5 is a Laravel (PHP) app backed by MySQL/MariaDB (it does NOT officially support Postgres); Valkey is wired for cache and queue. BEFORE first boot ROTATE: APP_KEY (must be a 32-char value; generate with `openssl rand -base64 32` and set APP_KEY=base64:<value>) and the MariaDB password (default 'invoiceninja'). APP_URL is pre-filled with {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} and must match the public URL. On first boot the entrypoint runs DB migrations and seeds the admin user from IN_USER_EMAIL / IN_PASSWORD (rotate IN_PASSWORD) - then log in at the app URL and change the password. PDF generation uses the bundled snappdf (headless Chromium) so no companion service is required; set PDF_GENERATOR=snappdf. REQUIRE_HTTPS=false is set because TLS is terminated at the ingress. Uploads, logos and generated PDF invoices persist under /var/www/html/public/storage on the mounted volume. SMTP for mail and optional S3 for storage are configured later via env vars or the Settings UI.

services.name: iomad
services.description: IOMAD is a multi-tenant Moodle distribution that turns Moodle into a SaaS e-learning platform for managing multiple companies, departments and clients.
services.installation: IOMAD is a Moodle distribution and requires MySQL/MariaDB (Moodle does not support PostgreSQL in these images), so this template ships the mariadb add-on. The actively-maintained dedicated image is elestio/iomad (rebuilt against current IOMAD releases). On first boot the container runs the Moodle/IOMAD installer against the database using the MYSQL_* env vars and provisions the admin account from ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD; set MOODLE_URL/SSL_PROXY are supplied so links resolve to the public HTTPS URL. After deploy, log in with the seeded admin and ROTATE: the ADMIN_PASSWORD env value, the MariaDB add-on password, and configure real SMTP under Site administration > Server > Email. Two persistent volumes are mounted: /var/www/html holds the IOMAD/Moodle code plus config.php, and /var/www/moodledata holds uploaded course files, caches and sessions. For production raise the app pod size (Moodle/PHP needs >=1Gi) and PHP memory_limit, and run Moodle cron. No external object store or search engine is required for a base install (add an external Elasticsearch/Solr later only if you enable global search).

services.name: iris
services.description: Open-source collaborative incident response platform for DFIR analysts to share investigation evidence, tasks, and timelines.
services.installation: DFIR-IRIS is a multi-container stack; this template runs the primary web/app server (ghcr.io/dfir-iris/iriswebapp_app) only. You MUST deploy these companion services to make it functional: (1) a worker from the SAME ghcr.io/dfir-iris/iriswebapp_app image with env IRIS_WORKER=1 (it runs the Celery jobs handler and shares this app's env, the postgres add-on, and the rabbitmq add-on broker). Upstream also ships (2) ghcr.io/dfir-iris/iriswebapp_db (a pre-tuned Postgres image) and (3) ghcr.io/dfir-iris/iriswebapp_nginx (a reverse proxy that terminates TLS on 443 and proxies to the app on 8000). Here the Kubero ingress terminates HTTP and the postgres add-on replaces the db image, so neither companion image is required; deploy iriswebapp_nginx only if you need its static-asset routing. Pin all companion images to the SAME tag as this app (v2.4.20). IMPORTANT: rotate IRIS_SECRET_KEY and IRIS_SECURITY_PASSWORD_SALT (each 'openssl rand -hex 32') and the postgres/rabbitmq passwords before production. The app auto-creates the schema and an initial 'administrator' user on first boot; the generated admin password is printed in the app pod logs (grep for 'WARNING :: post_init :: create_safe_admin'). User-uploaded case evidence is stored in the database, but server-side datastore files live on the mounted /home/iris/downloads volume.
services.license: GNU Lesser General Public License v3.0

services.name: itop
services.description: Combodo iTop: open-source ITSM and CMDB platform for incident, problem, change, service management and a configuration database.
services.installation: iTop is a PHP app that requires MySQL/MariaDB (it does NOT support Postgres); a 'mariadb' add-on is provisioned. The vbkunin/itop image is the de-facto community image (no official Combodo image exists); the '-base' tag is Apache+PHP only, without a bundled DB, so it uses the external MariaDB add-on. The database connection is NOT set via env vars - on first run open the URL and complete the web setup wizard, entering DB host 'itop-mysql' port 3306, user 'itop', password 'itop', db 'itop' (rotate this password from its default before going live). The wizard also creates the admin account. The MYSQL_PASS env only seeds the bundled MySQL of the non-base image and is unused here. Persist /var/www/html/data and the generated /var/www/html/conf so config and attachments survive restarts.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: jitsi
services.description: Self-hosted, end-to-end-encrypted video conferencing platform - a privacy-focused, fully featured alternative to Zoom and Google Meet.
services.installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Jitsi web frontend container ONLY (jitsi/web - an nginx that serves the Meet SPA on internal HTTP port 80 and reverse-proxies XMPP/BOSH). Jitsi Meet is a MULTI-CONTAINER stack and a working conference REQUIRES three more companion containers deployed as SEPARATE apps sharing the SAME config volume (/config) and the same generated secrets: (a) jitsi/prosody - the XMPP SIGNALING SERVER (this is the 'database': Jitsi stores all room/auth/presence state in Prosody, file-based, no SQL engine is used, which is why this template declares NO add-on); (b) jitsi/jicofo - the CONFERENCE FOCUS component that allocates conferences and manages the videobridge; (c) jitsi/jvb - the JITSI VIDEOBRIDGE that routes the actual WebRTC AUDIO/VIDEO media. All four containers must run the SAME JITSI image version. CRITICAL FIRST-RUN STEPS: 1) Generate the shared component passwords once - run './gen-passwords.sh' from the docker-jitsi-meet repo (or 'openssl rand -hex 16' for each) and set JICOFO_COMPONENT_SECRET, JICOFO_AUTH_PASSWORD, JVB_AUTH_PASSWORD identically across web/prosody/jicofo/jvb; the placeholder 'replace-with-openssl-rand-hex-16' values below MUST be rotated and copied to the other three pods. 2) Set PUBLIC_URL to your real https host (defaulted to the app URL token). 3) All pods mount the SAME /config (RWX) so Prosody can write generated certs/host config that web, jicofo and jvb read back. MEDIA/NETWORKING CAVEAT: jitsi/jvb needs UDP/10000 reachable from clients for WebRTC and DOCKER_HOST_ADDRESS / JVB_ADVERTISE_IPS set to the node's public IP - a vanilla pod cannot bind that without host networking or a node-level UDP path, so wire JVB's UDP port and advertised IP on the node before relying on it. ENABLE_LETSENCRYPT/ENABLE_HTTP_REDIRECT are left off because TLS is terminated at the Kubero ingress. Optional Jibri (jitsi/jibri) adds recording/streaming and needs extra kernel/audio access; omit for a basic deployment.

services.name: joplin
services.description: Self-hosted sync server for the Joplin open-source note-taking and to-do app, with end-to-end-encrypted multi-device sync and sharing.
services.installation: Deploy with the bundled PostgreSQL add-on; rotate the Postgres password (and after first login the admin password) from the template defaults before production. Joplin Server validates incoming requests against APP_BASE_URL, so it is set to the deployed public URL via {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} — if you change the hostname you must update this or clients get an 'invalid origin' error. The server listens on port 22300 (APP_PORT) and serves both the web admin UI and the sync API. Storage driver defaults to the database (DB_CLIENT=pg), so notes, attachments and blobs live in Postgres; the joplin-storage volume mounted at /home/joplin/storage is only used if you switch to the filesystem storage driver. On first run the database is migrated automatically; log in to the web UI with the default admin account admin@localhost / admin and change the password immediately, then point Joplin desktop/mobile clients at the URL using the 'Joplin Server' sync target. No external object store, search engine or SMTP is required for a base install (configure MAILER_* env vars later if you want email invitations).
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: keeweb
services.description: Free, cross-platform web password manager compatible with KeePass; opens and edits .kdbx files entirely in the browser.
services.installation: KeeWeb is a stateless, fully client-side password manager: the nginx container only serves static HTML/JS, and all cryptography (KeePass .kdbx parsing, AES/Argon2 key derivation) happens in the browser, so the server never sees your master password or plaintext. Because of this there is no database add-on and no server-side persistent volume - users open and save their .kdbx files from their own device or from an external storage provider they connect at runtime (WebDAV, Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, or generic file download/upload). To enable a storage provider you register an OAuth/app credential with that provider and configure it client-side; nothing needs to be seeded on the server. The official image listens on HTTP port 80 (and can self-sign TLS on 443) - here TLS is terminated at the Kubero ingress, so only port 80 is exposed. No SMTP, S3 or search engine is required. To self-host your own .kdbx over WebDAV, deploy a separate WebDAV server and point KeeWeb at its URL from the UI.

services.name: kener
services.description: Stunning, open-source status pages with incident management, multi-language support, monitors and customizable themes.
services.installation: Kener stores its data in a database that defaults to SQLite but is configured here to use the bundled PostgreSQL add-on via DATABASE_URL (rotate the postgres password from the default 'kener'). Set a strong KENER_SECRET_KEY before first start: replace the placeholder with `openssl rand -hex 32` and rotate it later if needed. ORIGIN is wired to the app's public URL so server-side calls and links resolve correctly. A 2Gi volume is mounted at /app/uploads to persist logos and uploaded assets; with Postgres selected, the /app/database SQLite directory is not required. First run: open the site and complete the setup/login, then add your monitors and customize site.json from the UI. No SMTP, S3 or external search engine is required.

services.name: kroki
services.description: Unified API that turns textual descriptions into diagrams (PlantUML, Mermaid, GraphViz, BPMN, C4, and many more).
services.installation: Kroki is a stateless rendering gateway and needs no database or persistent volume. The main yuzutech/kroki container renders most diagram types (PlantUML, GraphViz, C4, etc.) on its own. To enable Mermaid, BPMN and Excalidraw diagrams, deploy the optional companion containers (yuzutech/kroki-mermaid on :8002, yuzutech/kroki-bpmn on :8003, yuzutech/kroki-excalidraw on :8004) as separate apps and point the KROKI_*_HOST env vars at their in-cluster service names. The diagrams.net companion (yuzutech/kroki-diagramsnet) is experimental. Tune KROKI_MAX_URI_OPENING_COUNT / KROKI_MAX_URI_OPENING_TIMEOUT to harden against SSRF if exposing publicly.

services.name: lago
services.description: Open-source metering and usage-based billing platform for invoicing, subscriptions, and pay-as-you-go pricing.
services.installation: Lago is a multi-service Rails stack. This template runs the PRIMARY API server (getlago/api) on port 3000, wired to the Postgres and Valkey (Redis) add-ons provisioned here. You MUST deploy the companion services separately, all sharing the SAME database, Redis, and the env keys below: (1) Worker - image getlago/api with command './scripts/start.worker.sh' (Sidekiq background jobs, required for billing/invoicing to function); (2) Clock - image getlago/api with command './scripts/start.clock.sh' (recurring/scheduled jobs); (3) Frontend - image getlago/front on port 80, set its API_URL to this app's public URL and APP_ENV=production; (4) PDF generator - image getlago/lago-gotenberg:7.8.2 on port 3000, point LAGO_PDF_URL at it (default http://lago-pdf:3000). Lago's Postgres needs the partman + uuid-ossp extensions; if the provisioned Postgres lacks them, swap in getlago/postgres-partman:15-alpine. ROTATE before exposing: SECRET_KEY_BASE, LAGO_ENCRYPTION_PRIMARY_KEY, LAGO_ENCRYPTION_DETERMINISTIC_KEY, LAGO_ENCRYPTION_KEY_DERIVATION_SALT (each via 'openssl rand -hex 32'), LAGO_RSA_PRIVATE_KEY (a real PEM via 'openssl genrsa 2048 | base64'), and the Postgres add-on password. First run: the API container runs DB migrations on boot via LAGO_CREATE_ORG; create your first organization/user through the frontend signup. Object storage (S3) for invoice PDFs is optional and not provisioned - configure LAGO_USE_AWS_S3 + bucket env vars if needed.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: lemmy
services.description: Self-hosted, federated (ActivityPub) link aggregator and discussion forum - a Reddit-style alternative for the Fediverse.
services.installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Lemmy backend/API server ONLY (dessalines/lemmy, a Rust actix-web service listening on HTTP port 8536). Lemmy is a MULTI-CONTAINER stack and is NOT usable with this pod alone. CONFIG: Lemmy normally reads /config/config.hjson, but this template configures it purely via the LEMMY_* env vars - the critical ones are LEMMY_DATABASE_URL (points at the Postgres add-on) and LEMMY_HOSTNAME (your real public host, baked into ActivityPub actor URLs - set it BEFORE federating, it cannot be changed cleanly after going live). REQUIRED BEFORE FIRST BOOT: 1) Rotate the Postgres password. 2) The first user account you register through the UI becomes the instance admin - register it immediately after deploy. MANDATORY COMPANION SERVICES to deploy as SEPARATE Kubero apps: (A) FRONTEND - lemmy-ui, image dessalines/lemmy-ui:0.19.19, HTTP port 1234; set its env LEMMY_UI_LEMMY_INTERNAL_HOST to this backend's in-cluster service on :8536 and LEMMY_UI_LEMMY_EXTERNAL_HOST to your public host; PUBLIC INGRESS should be routed to lemmy-ui (port 1234), which proxies /api and /pictrs to the backend - the backend itself is an internal API. (B) IMAGE HOST - pict-rs, image asonix/pictrs:0.5, HTTP port 8080, with its own ~5Gi RWX volume at /mnt; point the backend at it via LEMMY_PICTRS__URL (e.g. http://lemmy-pictrs:8080/). (C) EMAIL (optional) - an SMTP relay such as mwader/postfix-relay for signup confirmation/notification mail; wire it via LEMMY_EMAIL__* or the smtp_server config. NOTE on datastores: Lemmy 0.19 needs ONLY PostgreSQL (>=15); it does NOT use Redis/Valkey. Federation, scheduled cleanup, and the outgoing activity queue all run inside this backend process - no separate worker/scheduler container is required.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: lightdash
services.description: Open-source BI platform that turns your dbt project into a self-serve analytics tool with explores, charts, and dashboards.
services.installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Lightdash web/api server (lightdash/lightdash, port 8080), which on boot also runs its own DB migrations against the bundled Postgres add-on. To be fully functional you MUST ALSO deploy a companion HEADLESS BROWSER deployment from image `ghcr.io/browserless/chromium:latest` (formerly browserless/chrome), exposing port 3000 on an in-cluster service named to match HEADLESS_BROWSER_HOST below; it renders charts/dashboards for scheduled deliveries, image downloads, and PDF exports. Lightdash also needs S3-COMPATIBLE OBJECT STORAGE for results/file caching and scheduled-delivery artifacts — point S3_ENDPOINT/S3_BUCKET/S3_REGION/S3_ACCESS_KEY/S3_SECRET_KEY at the RustFS S3 add-on or any S3 bucket (omitted from add-ons because it is not one of the supported operator datastores). ROTATE before exposing: LIGHTDASH_SECRET (`openssl rand -base64 32`; do NOT change it after data exists or saved warehouse credentials become undecryptable) and the Postgres add-on password (keep PGPASSWORD in sync). Set SITE_URL via the {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} token (already wired). The first user to register becomes the admin/owner. Optional outbound email needs the EMAIL_SMTP_* env vars (not provisioned).

services.name: lightldap
services.description: Lightweight authentication server providing a simplified, opinionated LDAP interface with a clean web UI for user and group management.
services.installation: LLDAP exposes its web admin UI on port 17170 and an LDAP endpoint on port 3890 (LDAP is not HTTP; expose it separately if other apps must bind to it). ROTATE these template defaults before production: LLDAP_JWT_SECRET and LLDAP_KEY_SEED (generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`), and LLDAP_LDAP_USER_PASS (the initial admin password for the bind user 'admin'). On first run log in at the app URL with admin / the LLDAP_LDAP_USER_PASS value, then create users/groups. This spec uses the bundled Postgres add-on via LLDAP_DATABASE_URL; if you prefer the default embedded SQLite store, remove the postgres add-on and LLDAP_DATABASE_URL and keep the /data volume. Adjust LLDAP_LDAP_BASE_DN to your domain.

services.name: magento
services.description: Open-source PHP e-commerce platform with a flexible catalog, checkout, promotions and a powerful admin for building online stores.
services.installation: Bitnami Magento image. The app web container is the PRIMARY service; companion services you must run alongside it: (1) a search engine - Magento 2.4+ REQUIRES Elasticsearch 7/8 or OpenSearch 2.x, which is NOT a provided operator add-on, so deploy it separately (e.g. image bitnami/opensearch or opensearchproject/opensearch) and point MAGENTO_ELASTICSEARCH_HOST/ELASTICSEARCH_HOST at it on port 9200; (2) optional but recommended cron/queue worker running the same bitnami/magento image with command 'php /opt/bitnami/magento/htdocs/bin/magento cron:run' for scheduled tasks, indexers and async order processing. MariaDB stores all catalog/order data; Valkey (Redis-compatible) is wired for page cache and session storage. First run auto-installs Magento and creates the admin user from MAGENTO_USERNAME/MAGENTO_PASSWORD/MAGENTO_EMAIL - rotate MAGENTO_PASSWORD (currently a placeholder), the MariaDB password, and admin email before exposing publicly. Admin panel lives at {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}/admin. Install is slow (several minutes) and memory-heavy; give the web pod >=2Gi RAM (PHP memory_limit). Reindex with 'bin/magento indexer:reindex' after first boot.

services.name: mantisbt
services.description: MantisBT is an open source web-based bug and issue tracker that balances simplicity with powerful project and workflow management.
services.installation: Database is configured through the first-run web installer at /admin/install.php, NOT via env vars. To reach it, this template sets MANTIS_ENABLE_ADMIN=1 so the /admin directory is accessible; SET IT BACK TO 0 after setup for security. At install.php choose database type 'MySQL Improved (mysqli)' and enter: Hostname=mantisbt-mysql, Username=mantisbt, Password=mantisbt, Database name=mantisbt (these are template defaults from the mariadb add-on; rotate them). The default app admin login is administrator / root: change the password immediately after install. Persisted volumes hold config (/var/www/html/config) and custom files/uploads (/var/www/html/custom).

services.name: mastodon
services.description: Free, open-source decentralized social network server based on ActivityPub - self-hosted microblogging for the Fediverse.
services.installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Mastodon web (Puma/Rails) container from ghcr.io/mastodon/mastodon, HTTP on port 3000. Mastodon is a MULTI-CONTAINER stack: this single template covers ONLY the web server, so you MUST deploy these companion services separately for a working instance: 1) SIDEKIQ WORKER - same image ghcr.io/mastodon/mastodon:v4.4.18 with command ['bundle','exec','sidekiq'] and the SAME env vars as this web container; this runs ALL background queues (federation push/pull, mailers, media processing, scheduled tasks) and is MANDATORY - without it nothing federates. 2) STREAMING SERVER - image ghcr.io/mastodon/mastodon-streaming:v4.4.18 listening on port 4000 (the real-time WebSocket/timeline API); route /api/v1/streaming to it. Both companions share the same Postgres + Redis add-ons created here. REQUIRED BEFORE FIRST BOOT (rotate every placeholder): generate the cryptographic secrets - run 'docker run --rm ghcr.io/mastodon/mastodon:v4.4.18 bundle exec rake secret' to produce SECRET_KEY_BASE and OTP_SECRET; run 'bundle exec rake mastodon:webpush:generate_vapid_key' for VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY/VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY; and run 'bundle exec rails db:encryption:init' for the three ACTIVE_RECORD_ENCRYPTION_* keys. Set LOCAL_DOMAIN to your real public host BEFORE federating - it is baked into ActivityPub actor URLs and cannot be changed after going live. FIRST-RUN SETUP: exec into the web pod and run 'RAILS_ENV=production bundle exec rake db:setup' (creates schema + seeds), then create an owner account with 'bin/tootctl accounts create <user> --email <email> --confirmed --role Owner'. EMAIL: set SMTP_* env vars (not preset here) so confirmation/notification mail works. OPTIONAL EXTERNALS (omitted from addons, wire up later): full-text search needs Elasticsearch/OpenSearch (set ES_ENABLED=true + ES_HOST/ES_PORT and run 'tootctl search deploy'); media at scale should use an S3-compatible bucket (e.g. the RustFS add-on) via S3_ENABLED=true + S3_BUCKET/AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID/AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY instead of the local volume.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: mautic
services.description: Open-source marketing automation platform for email campaigns, lead management, landing pages, and multichannel customer journeys.
services.installation: Mautic only supports MySQL/MariaDB (no Postgres). This template deploys the primary mautic_web container; on first run open the URL and complete the web installer, or rely on the auto-install env vars (set DOCKER_MAUTIC_LOAD_TEST_DATA=false). IMPORTANT: rotate the mariadb password and the MAUTIC_DB_PASSWORD env to match before going to production. For full functionality deploy two companion containers from the SAME image mautic/mautic:5-apache, changing only DOCKER_MAUTIC_ROLE: (1) a worker container with DOCKER_MAUTIC_ROLE=mautic_worker (consumes the Symfony Messenger queues for email sending/processing), and (2) a cron container with DOCKER_MAUTIC_ROLE=mautic_cron (runs scheduled segment/campaign/email cron jobs). All three containers must share the same MAUTIC_DB_* settings and the same persistent volume (/var/www/html). Redis/Valkey can optionally back the Messenger transport and cache for higher throughput but is not required.

services.name: maybe
services.description: Open-source personal finance and wealth management app to track accounts, budgets, investments and net worth.
services.installation: Rails app requiring PostgreSQL and Redis (deployed as add-ons). Rotate SECRET_KEY_BASE to a unique value (openssl rand -hex 64) and rotate the postgres password before exposing publicly. A separate Sidekiq worker process (same image, command: bundle exec sidekiq) should be deployed for background jobs; the web container will function for basic use without it but recurring syncs/imports need the worker. Set SELF_HOSTING_ENABLED=true. If serving over plain HTTP behind the ingress's own TLS, RAILS_FORCE_SSL/RAILS_ASSUME_SSL are left false; enable them if terminating TLS at the app.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: mediacms
services.description: Modern, fully featured open-source video and media content management system for building self-hosted media portals and streaming sites.
services.installation: This template runs the PRIMARY MediaCMS application container ONLY (mediacms/mediacms). MediaCMS is a Django + Celery MULTI-CONTAINER stack and is NOT complete with this pod alone. By default this pod is configured as the WEB front end (ENABLE_UWSGI=yes, ENABLE_NGINX=yes serving HTTP on port 80), with migrations and Celery DISABLED so they can run as dedicated apps. REQUIRED before/at first boot: 1) Run database migrations ONCE - deploy a one-shot companion app from the SAME image mediacms/mediacms with env ENABLE_UWSGI=no, ENABLE_NGINX=no, ENABLE_CELERY_BEAT=no, ENABLE_CELERY_SHORT=no, ENABLE_CELERY_LONG=no, ENABLE_MIGRATIONS=yes, ADMIN_USER=admin, ADMIN_EMAIL=admin@localhost and ADMIN_PASSWORD set to a strong value (this also creates the first superuser). Alternatively set ENABLE_MIGRATIONS=yes on THIS web pod for the very first deploy, then set it back to no. 2) Rotate the Postgres password (template default is the slug 'mediacms') and set a real ADMIN_PASSWORD. Valkey/Redis is password-less in-cluster. MANDATORY COMPANION SERVICES to deploy as SEPARATE apps from the SAME image mediacms/mediacms, sharing the SAME Postgres + Valkey + the /home/mediacms/mediacms/media_files volume (use RWX storage so uploads/transcodes are visible to all): (a) Celery WORKER for short tasks - env ENABLE_UWSGI=no, ENABLE_NGINX=no, ENABLE_CELERY_SHORT=yes, ENABLE_CELERY_LONG=yes, ENABLE_CELERY_BEAT=no, ENABLE_MIGRATIONS=no (handles thumbnailing, encoding/transcoding and notifications - REQUIRED or uploaded videos never finish processing); (b) Celery BEAT scheduler - env ENABLE_CELERY_BEAT=yes with all other ENABLE_* = no (periodic cleanup/maintenance jobs). All companion pods need the identical POSTGRES_* and REDIS_LOCATION env vars as this web pod. Route public ingress to THIS web pod (it runs the bundled nginx). Set FRONTEND_HOST to your public https URL so absolute media/asset links resolve correctly. Heavy video transcoding is CPU/RAM intensive - size the worker pod accordingly. For production at scale media_files can be offloaded to S3 (e.g. the RustFS add-on) via Django storages, but the default uses the local volume.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: metabase-postgres
services.description: Open-source business intelligence and analytics tool to query, visualize, and share dashboards from your databases without writing SQL.
services.installation: This template runs the official metabase/metabase image and stores all application state (questions, dashboards, users, saved data-source credentials, settings) in the bundled PostgreSQL add-on via the MB_DB_* variables, so no persistent volume is needed. Rotate the Postgres add-on password before exposing. CRITICAL: set MB_ENCRYPTION_SECRET_KEY to a fixed random value (openssl rand -base64 32) and NEVER change it after first boot, because it encrypts the credentials of the external databases you connect to; if it is lost or changed, those saved connections become undecryptable. MB_SITE_URL is wired to the public URL token; keep it correct or password-reset and embedding links will break. On first run, open the app and complete the setup wizard to create the initial admin account. Outbound email (alerts, invites, pulses) requires SMTP settings configured in Admin > Email (not provisioned here). To analyze data, add your own external database connections from the admin panel after setup.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: minthcm
services.description: Open-source, AI-native Human Capital Management (HCM) platform for HR processes, built on the SuiteCRM core with full data ownership.
services.installation: MintHCM requires a companion Elasticsearch 7.10+ service for search/indexing (not an operator add-on here) - deploy one and point ELASTICSEARCH_HOST/PORT at it, or the app boots but search features stay disabled. The bundled MariaDB add-on holds all relational data; rotate the default db password (currently the slug 'minthcm') after first deploy. On first run the entrypoint installs the schema using MINT_USER/MINT_PASS (admin/minthcm) and MINT_URL ({{KUBERO_APP_URL}}) - log in and change the admin password immediately. Set INSTALL_DEMO_DATA=yes only for evaluation. Persistent app code, config and uploads live under /var/www/html.
