services.name: misskey
services.description: Decentralized, ActivityPub-federated microblogging platform with reactions, Drive file storage and a customizable UI.
services.installation: Misskey is configured PRIMARILY via a YAML file (.config/default.yml), not env vars alone, so before first boot you MUST supply that config (mounted at /misskey/.config) pointing db.host=misskey-postgresql-rw / db.port=5432 / db.db=misskey and redis.host=rfr-misskey-valkey-readwrite / redis.port=6379, and set url to your public https URL. The env vars below mirror those values and cover the parts Misskey reads from the environment; rotate POSTGRES_PASSWORD and the db password in default.yml to match. After the web container is up, run the DB migrations (it runs `pnpm migrate` on start in the official image). COMPANION SERVICES: the single misskey/misskey image runs web + the federation/inbox/deliver/queue workers in one process by default, so no extra worker container is strictly required for small instances; for scale you can run additional misskey/misskey replicas with MK_ONLY_QUEUE/MK_ONLY_SERVER style splits. OPTIONAL: for full-text post search Misskey can use Elasticsearch/OpenSearch or Meilisearch (image getmeili/meilisearch) configured in default.yml under meilisearch/elasticsearch - not provisioned here; and for object storage of Drive uploads it can use any S3-compatible bucket (configured under objectStorage in default.yml) instead of the local volume.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: moodle
services.description: Moodle is the world's most popular open-source learning management system (LMS) for building online courses, classrooms and e-learning.
services.installation: Moodle requires MySQL/MariaDB (the maintained container images target MariaDB, not PostgreSQL), so this template ships the mariadb add-on. The image is elestio/moodle, an actively-rebuilt Verified Publisher image tracking current Moodle releases (Bitnami's moodle image moved behind a paywall in 2025 and is no longer freely pullable). On first boot the container runs the Moodle installer against the database using the MOODLE_DATABASE_* env vars and provisions the admin account from MOODLE_USERNAME/MOODLE_PASSWORD/MOODLE_EMAIL; MOODLE_HOST and MOODLE_REVERSEPROXY are set so generated links resolve to the public HTTPS URL behind the ingress. After deploy, log in with the seeded admin and ROTATE: the MOODLE_PASSWORD env value, the MariaDB add-on password, and configure real SMTP under Site administration > Server > Email. Two persistent volumes are mounted: /bitnami/moodle holds the Moodle code plus config.php, and /bitnami/moodledata holds uploaded course files, caches and sessions. For production raise the app pod size (Moodle/PHP needs >=1Gi) and PHP memory_limit, and schedule the Moodle cron (admin/cli/cron.php) every minute. Optional companions: deploy the memcached add-on and point Moodle's MUC application cache at it for performance, and add an external Solr/Elasticsearch only if you enable global search (neither is required for a base install).

services.name: neko-rooms
services.description: Self-hosted room management system for n.eko virtual browsers, a rabb.it alternative for collaborative co-browsing and watch-together sessions.
services.installation: Neko Rooms spins up and tears down n.eko (virtual browser) containers on demand and exposes a management UI for them. It keeps no SQL database: room metadata and persistent room storage live on disk, so the bundled volume is mounted at /data (set NEKO_ROOMS_STORAGE_INTERNAL/EXTERNAL there) and no add-on is required. IMPORTANT runtime caveat: neko-rooms is built around the Docker host model and orchestrates SIBLING containers via the Docker socket (/var/run/docker.sock) plus a WebRTC UDP port range (NEKO_ROOMS_EPR, default 59000-59049) and NAT1TO1 host IP. A vanilla Kubernetes pod has no Docker socket and cannot bind that wide UDP range, so to actually launch rooms this must run with access to a Docker/containerd daemon (e.g. a node-level Docker socket mount or a DinD sidecar) and the EPR range exposed on the node - plan node/host wiring before relying on it. Set NEKO_ROOMS_INSTANCE_URL/NAT1TO1 to your real public host. There is no built-in user database; protect the admin UI with NEKO_ROOMS_HTTP_AUTH or front it with an auth proxy and rotate any auth secret you set. No SMTP, S3 or external search engine is needed.

services.name: nopcommerce
services.description: Open-source ASP.NET Core e-commerce platform and shopping cart for building online stores.
services.installation: First run launches the nopCommerce install wizard at the app URL: pick the PostgreSQL provider and enter the connection details below (host nopcommerce-postgresql-rw, port 5432, db/user/pass = nopcommerce). The wizard creates the admin user you specify and writes config to /app/App_Data (persisted via the data volume). Rotate the default postgres password after install. nopCommerce can run without the DB add-on against SQL Server/MySQL too, but this template ships PostgreSQL. Optional: front with a CDN/object storage for media at scale; not required to boot.

services.name: odoo
services.description: Open-source suite of business apps: CRM, e-commerce, accounting, inventory, project management, HR and more.
services.installation: Odoo requires PostgreSQL ONLY (it does not support MySQL); the postgresql add-on is wired in via HOST/USER/PASSWORD/PORT. On first run the role 'odoo' must be able to CREATE DATABASE so the web installer can provision the Odoo database; the bundled add-on role has this. Rotate the add-on password and the 'admin' master password (set ODOO_RC or use the Database Manager) after install, and lock down /web/database/manager. The /var/lib/odoo volume holds the filestore (attachments, uploads, sessions) and must be persistent. Custom modules go in /mnt/extra-addons. COMPANION SERVICES (deploy as separate apps from the same odoo:18.0 image, sharing the same PostgreSQL add-on and filestore volume): (1) a long-polling/gevent worker for live chat, discuss and websockets, started with command 'odoo gevent' (or 'odoo --workers=N' in multi-worker mode listening on the gevent port 8072); (2) optionally a dedicated cron/scheduler worker (odoo --max-cron-threads=N). In a single-replica deployment the embedded cron and HTTP worker handle scheduling, so the standalone web template alone is functional for evaluation. For production multi-worker mode put a reverse proxy in front (proxy_mode=True) so X-Forwarded-* is honored.
services.license: GNU Lesser General Public License v3.0

services.name: onlyoffice
services.description: OnlyOffice document collaboration on Nextcloud: file sync and sharing with users and an admin portal, plus real-time editing of documents, spreadsheets and presentations via a pre-wired OnlyOffice connector.
services.installation: Deploys the stackblaze/nextcloud-onlyoffice image (Nextcloud with the ONLYOFFICE connector, self-configured on first boot from DOCUMENT_SERVER_URL + ONLYOFFICE_JWT_SECRET) plus PostgreSQL and Valkey add-ons. Point DOCUMENT_SERVER_URL at an OnlyOffice Docs server and set ONLYOFFICE_JWT_SECRET to its JWT_SECRET (they must match). Admin = NEXTCLOUD_ADMIN_USER / NEXTCLOUD_ADMIN_PASSWORD (change it). Data lives under /var/www/html.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: onlyoffice-docs
services.description: OnlyOffice Docs (Document Server) - the editor engine for real-time collaborative editing of documents, spreadsheets and presentations. A backend host apps (Nextcloud, custom integrations) connect to; it has no UI of its own.
services.installation: The official onlyoffice/documentserver image with bundled PostgreSQL, RabbitMQ and Valkey add-ons. JWT signing is ON by default - configure the host app with the same JWT_SECRET (replace the placeholder, openssl rand -hex 32). No admin UI; the app URL shows a welcome/healthcheck page. Use this for a dedicated Document Server (e.g. a backend for the OnlyOffice / Nextcloud template).
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: openobserve
services.description: Rust-based, petabyte-scale observability platform for logs, metrics, traces and real user monitoring; a lightweight Elasticsearch/Datadog alternative.
services.installation: This template runs the official single-node (local mode) image openobserve/openobserve on HTTP port 5080. DATASTORE: in local mode OpenObserve stores its metadata in an embedded SQLite DB and its ingested log/metric/trace data on local disk, so NO external database add-on is provisioned - everything lives on the mounted /data volume (ZO_DATA_DIR). For that reason the persistent volume MUST be retained across redeploys or all ingested data is lost; grow the volume to match your retention. BEFORE FIRST RUN you MUST rotate ZO_ROOT_USER_EMAIL and ZO_ROOT_USER_PASSWORD - these create the initial admin account on first boot (password must be 8-128 chars with upper, lower, digit and special char). Log in at the root URL with those credentials to get your ingestion token. SCALE-OUT (NOT configured here): for HA/cluster mode OpenObserve instead needs an external PostgreSQL or MySQL metastore (ZO_META_STORE=postgres) plus S3-compatible object storage (ZO_LOCAL_MODE=false, ZO_LOCAL_MODE_STORAGE=s3 with ZO_S3_* settings) and the multi-role router/querier/ingester/compactor topology - point those at the postgres add-on and an external S3 bucket if you outgrow a single node. Telemetry is disabled via ZO_TELEMETRY=false.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: opnform
services.description: Beautiful open-source form builder to create and share forms, surveys and quizzes with no code, a self-hosted alternative to Typeform and Google Forms.
services.installation: Multi-container Laravel (PHP/API) + Nuxt (Vue/client) stack. THIS template deploys the PRIMARY API/server image jhumanj/opnform-api:latest (Laravel + nginx + php-fpm on port 80), which serves the REST API and runs DB migrations on boot. Rotate APP_KEY (base64:$(openssl rand -base64 32)), JWT_SECRET (openssl rand -hex 32), and the Postgres password (default 'opnform') before exposing publicly. Companion services to deploy separately, ALL from the SAME jhumanj/opnform-api:latest image, sharing this template's env: (1) api-worker -- override command to run the queue worker `php artisan queue:work`; (2) api-scheduler -- run the cron scheduler `php artisan schedule:work`. Also deploy the FRONTEND as a separate app from jhumanj/opnform-client:latest (Nuxt SSR, listens on port 3000) with NUXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE pointing at this api's /api URL. The reference compose puts an nginx ingress in front that routes / to the client and /api,/forms,/storage to this api; replicate that routing at the Kubero ingress (path-based) or front both with a small nginx. Postgres (DB_CONNECTION=pgsql) and Valkey/Redis add-ons are bundled here. Set FRONTEND_URL to the public client URL after deploying it. Local file uploads/avatars/exports persist on the /usr/share/nginx/html/storage/app volume; for production object storage set FILESYSTEM_DISK=s3 and add S3-compatible storage env (no S3 add-on is provisioned by this template). Configure SMTP (MAIL_* env) for outbound email after install.

services.name: owncast
services.description: Self-hosted live video streaming and chat server, a free and open-source alternative to services like Twitch.
services.installation: Owncast stores all state (SQLite database, config, uploaded logos/emojis, and HLS video segments) under /app/data, so no external datastore add-on is required; the persistent volume is mandatory. On first run open the web UI, then visit Admin (/admin, default login admin / abc123) and immediately change the stream key and admin password. Point your broadcasting software (OBS, etc.) at rtmp://<host>:1935/live using your stream key. For production scale you may offload HLS segments to S3-compatible object storage and front it with a CDN, configured in the admin Storage settings (not an operator add-on).

services.name: owncloud
services.description: ownCloud is a self-hosted file sync and share platform for storing, accessing and collaborating on your files across devices.
services.installation: The official owncloud/server image initializes itself on first boot using the OWNCLOUD_ADMIN_* and OWNCLOUD_DB_* environment variables, so no manual setup wizard is needed. It is built and tested against MariaDB/MySQL plus Redis (provided here by the mariadb and valkey add-ons). Rotate the mariadb add-on credentials and the OWNCLOUD_ADMIN_PASSWORD ('owncloud' default) after first login. OWNCLOUD_TRUSTED_DOMAINS / OWNCLOUD_DOMAIN are wired to the app host so the instance accepts requests at its public URL; add any extra domains there. The valkey add-on is password-less, so OWNCLOUD_REDIS_HOST points at rfr-owncloud-valkey-readwrite with no password set. User files, config and the SQLite-free data dir live under /mnt/data (persisted by the owncloud-data volume). For large instances raise the app pod size and PHP memory limit. An external object/S3 store is optional and not required for a base install.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: papercups
services.description: Open-source live chat and customer messaging platform (Intercom alternative) for support, onboarding, and team inboxes.
services.installation: Deploy with the bundled PostgreSQL add-on. Rotate SECRET_KEY_BASE (generate with `openssl rand -base64 48`) and the Postgres password from their template defaults before going to production. DATABASE_URL uses the ecto:// scheme and points at the in-cluster postgres add-on; REQUIRE_DB_SSL is false because the add-on speaks plaintext inside the cluster. The app serves the backend and web UI on port 4000. On first run the database migrations run automatically, then open the app and register the first account, which becomes the admin. Set BACKEND_URL to the deployed host so the chat widget and email links resolve correctly. File uploads (REACT_APP_FILE_UPLOADS_ENABLED) require an external S3-compatible bucket plus AWS_* / BUCKET_NAME env vars; outbound email requires a Mailgun or SMTP provider config. Neither S3 nor SMTP is provisioned here.

services.name: papermerge
services.description: Self-hosted document management system (DMS) for digital archives: scan, OCR, organize, tag and full-text-search PDFs and scanned documents.
services.installation: Deploy with the bundled PostgreSQL and Valkey (Redis) add-ons; rotate the Postgres password and the random PAPERMERGE__SECURITY__SECRET_KEY placeholder (openssl rand -hex 32), and change the bootstrap superuser password PAPERMERGE__AUTH__PASSWORD before going to production. The web container listens on internal port 80 (apache) and serves both the React UI and the REST API. Uploaded documents and OCR output live under /db/media (PAPERMERGE__MAIN__MEDIA_ROOT) on the persistent volume. On first boot the database is migrated automatically and a superuser is created from PAPERMERGE__AUTH__USERNAME / PAPERMERGE__AUTH__PASSWORD; log in and change the password immediately. OCR is performed by separate companion worker images that must be deployed alongside this app and share the same Postgres, Redis and media volume: papermerge/ocrworker:0.3.1 (OCR), papermerge/i3worker:0.3 (full-text index writer), papermerge/path-tmpl-worker:0.4.1 (path templates). Full-text search defaults to a built-in SQLite/Tantivy index (PAPERMERGE__SEARCH__URL unset) so no external Elasticsearch is required for a single-node install; set PAPERMERGE__SEARCH__URL only if you front it with Solr/OpenSearch. If you switch document storage to S3 instead of the local volume, also deploy papermerge/s3worker and set PAPERMERGE__S3__BUCKET_NAME.

services.name: parseable
services.description: Rust-based, S3-native log analytics and observability platform with a built-in web console; a lightweight Elasticsearch/Splunk alternative.
services.installation: This template runs the official parseable/parseable image in single-node LOCAL-STORE mode via the custom command 'parseable local-store', serving the HTTP API and web console on port 8000. DATASTORE: in local-store mode Parseable persists all ingested log data and metadata on disk (P_FS_DIR=/parseable/data) plus an in-flight staging area (P_STAGING_DIR=/parseable/staging), so NO external database add-on is provisioned - everything lives on the mounted volumes. For that reason these persistent volumes MUST be retained across redeploys or ingested data is lost; grow them to match your retention. BEFORE FIRST RUN you MUST rotate P_USERNAME and P_PASSWORD - these create the initial admin login (the defaults admin/admin are insecure); log in at the root URL with those credentials. PRODUCTION SCALE-OUT (NOT configured here): for durable, horizontally-scalable storage Parseable instead writes to S3-compatible object storage - run 'parseable s3-store' and set P_S3_URL, P_S3_ACCESS_KEY, P_S3_SECRET_KEY, P_S3_REGION and P_S3_BUCKET to point at an external S3 bucket; this template uses local disk for simplicity. Send logs over HTTP to /api/v1/ingest with your credentials.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: passit
services.description: Open-source, self-hosted password manager with end-to-end encryption and team sharing, plus browser extensions and a REST API.
services.installation: Image: passit/passit:stable (official, on Docker Hub; project is hosted on GitLab). This is a Django app. ROTATE SECRET_KEY (set it to `openssl rand -hex 32`) and the Postgres password (default 'passit') before exposing publicly. ALLOWED_HOSTS is set to '*' for first boot; tighten it to your real host. The container runs Django migrations on start, then serves via gunicorn on port 5000. Create the first admin/user via the web sign-up flow (registration is open by default; disable open registration with SIGN_UP_ENABLED=false after creating your accounts). DATABASE_URL points at the bundled postgres add-on (passit-postgresql-rw) and REDIS_URL at the valkey add-on (used for Celery background jobs / caching). For password-reset and invite emails, configure SMTP via EMAIL_* / SERVER_EMAIL env vars or the Django settings after install. No object storage required.

services.name: peertube
services.description: Free, federated (ActivityPub) video hosting and live-streaming platform - a self-hosted, decentralized alternative to YouTube.
services.installation: This template runs the PRIMARY PeerTube server container ONLY (chocobozzz/peertube, a Node.js app listening on HTTP port 9000). REQUIRED before first boot: 1) Rotate PEERTUBE_SECRET - generate with 'openssl rand -hex 32' and replace the placeholder; this signs sessions/JWT and CANNOT be left at the default. 2) Set PEERTUBE_WEBSERVER_HOSTNAME to your real public host BEFORE going live - it is baked into ActivityPub actor URLs and federated video URLs and cannot be changed cleanly once you have federated. 3) Note the default DB suffix is empty here (PEERTUBE_DB_SUFFIX="") so the add-on database 'peertube' is used as-is; the upstream compose default appends '_prod'. 4) After first start, find the auto-generated root admin password in the logs (search for 'Username: root') or set it with 'NODE_CONFIG_DIR=/config NODE_ENV=production npm run reset-password -- -u root' inside the pod. COMPANION/EXTERNAL SERVICES (NOT bundled): (a) EMAIL - PeerTube needs an SMTP server for account verification, password reset and notifications. Deploy a separate mail relay (e.g. the official 'mwader/postfix-relay' image) and point PEERTUBE_SMTP_HOSTNAME/PEERTUBE_SMTP_PORT at it, or set PEERTUBE_SMTP_* to an external provider; mail is disabled until configured. (b) OBJECT STORAGE (recommended at scale) - videos, thumbnails and HLS playlists can grow huge; for production set PEERTUBE_OBJECT_STORAGE_ENABLED=true plus the PEERTUBE_OBJECT_STORAGE_* S3 vars (endpoint, region, bucket, access/secret keys) against an external S3 bucket (e.g. the RustFS add-on) instead of the local /data volume. (c) Transcoding, federation queues and scheduled jobs all run INSIDE this single container (handled by internal Bull/Redis job queues), so no separate worker/scheduler pod is required - but transcoding is CPU/RAM heavy, so give this pod a large pod size and persistent /data. Rotate the Postgres password; Valkey/Redis is password-less in-cluster (PEERTUBE_REDIS_AUTH is left blank). The /data volume must be persistent (and RWX if you ever scale replicas) because uploaded/transcoded videos live there until moved to object storage.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: penpot
services.description: Open-source design and prototyping platform for product teams; the design tool for design and code collaboration.
services.installation: Penpot is a MULTI-CONTAINER stack. This template deploys the PRIMARY web entrypoint, the nginx FRONTEND image `penpotapp/frontend` (HTTP 8080), which serves the SPA and reverse-proxies API/asset traffic to the backend and exporter. By itself the frontend will not function until you also deploy these COMPANION services as their own Kubero apps (all pinned to the SAME version tag 2.16): (1) BACKEND -> image `penpotapp/backend` (listens on HTTP 6060); give it the database/redis/secret env shown below: PENPOT_DATABASE_URI=postgresql://penpot-postgresql-rw:5432/penpot, PENPOT_DATABASE_USERNAME=penpot, PENPOT_DATABASE_PASSWORD=penpot, PENPOT_REDIS_URI=redis://rfr-penpot-valkey-readwrite:6379/0, PENPOT_SECRET_KEY=(rotate, `openssl rand -hex 32`), PENPOT_PUBLIC_URI={{KUBERO_APP_URL}}, PENPOT_FLAGS, and the assets-fs storage env; it must MOUNT THE SAME shared assets volume at /opt/data/assets as the frontend. (2) EXPORTER -> image `penpotapp/exporter` (HTTP 6061), set PENPOT_PUBLIC_URI=http://penpot-frontend:8080 and PENPOT_REDIS_URI to the same Valkey URI; it renders thumbnails/exports via a headless browser. The frontend reaches the backend/exporter by the env PENPOT_BACKEND_URI / PENPOT_EXPORTER_URI (or PENPOT_INTERNAL_RESOLVER) -> point these at the backend (`http://penpot-backend:6060`) and exporter (`http://penpot-exporter:6061`) service names once those apps are up. ADD-ONS provisioned: a Postgres add-on (Penpot requires PostgreSQL) and a Valkey/Redis add-on. ROTATE the default Postgres password and set a strong PENPOT_SECRET_KEY (the same value MUST be shared by frontend, backend and exporter). FIRST RUN: by default registration is open; after creating the first owner account add `disable-registration` to PENPOT_FLAGS. SMTP is optional via PENPOT_SMTP_* env (with the `enable-smtp` flag); without it user emails print to the backend log. User-uploaded assets live on the shared local volume at /opt/data/assets (for multi-replica or large deployments use an S3-compatible backend instead, set via PENPOT_ASSETS_STORAGE_BACKEND=assets-s3, which is NOT provisioned here).

services.name: photon
services.description: Open-source geocoder for OpenStreetMap data with search-as-you-type and reverse geocoding, powered by an embedded OpenSearch index.
services.installation: Photon is a self-contained geocoder: it bundles its own OpenSearch/Elasticsearch search engine and serves an HTTP API on port 2322 (no external database add-on is needed). It requires a pre-built search index. The rtuszik/photon-docker image used here automatically downloads the GraphHopper index dump for the configured REGION on first start and keeps it updated. IMPORTANT: index dumps are very large (a country like 'germany' is several GB; the worldwide 'planet' index is ~90-200GB) and the initial download/extraction can take a long time, so size the /photon/data volume to match your chosen REGION (the 1Gi default below is only enough for a tiny region/testing - increase it before deploying a real region). Set REGION to a country/continent code (e.g. 'germany', 'europe') or 'planet' for worldwide. Query the API at /api?q=<text> and reverse-geocode at /reverse?lon=<x>&lat=<y>. No login or secrets to rotate. To pin a version instead of 'latest', use one of the rtuszik/photon-docker release tags.

services.name: photoprism
services.description: AI-powered, self-hosted photo and video management app that organizes your library with automatic tagging, face recognition, and search.
services.installation: Deploy with the bundled MariaDB add-on (PhotoPrism recommends MariaDB over SQLite for performance). On first run, log in as 'admin' with the password set in PHOTOPRISM_ADMIN_PASSWORD and rotate it immediately. Rotate the MariaDB password and PHOTOPRISM_ADMIN_PASSWORD from their template defaults. Upload originals via the web UI or place them under /photoprism/originals; the /photoprism/storage volume holds the sidecar/cache/index data and must be persistent. Indexing and face recognition run in the background after import. For large libraries, increase PHOTOPRISM_WORKERS or the app pod size.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: phpbb
services.description: phpBB is a free, open-source flat-forum bulletin board software for building and running an online community discussion board.
services.installation: Uses the actively-maintained bitnami/phpbb image, which bundles Apache + PHP and only supports MariaDB/MySQL (phpBB does support PostgreSQL upstream, but this image does not), so the mariadb add-on is shipped. On first boot the container runs the phpBB installer non-interactively against the MariaDB add-on, creating the admin account from PHPBB_USERNAME / PHPBB_PASSWORD / PHPBB_EMAIL. ROTATE the admin password (PHPBB_PASSWORD, currently a placeholder) and the mariadb add-on credentials (db/user/password all default to 'phpbb') before going to production, then log in at /adm to harden settings. PHPBB_HOST is wired to the public host via {{KUBERO_APP_HOST}} so generated links use the live URL. Persistent data (config, uploads/attachments, avatars, cache) lives under /bitnami/phpbb on the mounted volume. Configure SMTP via the board's ACP or PHPBB_SMTP_* env vars for outbound email. ALLOW_EMPTY_PASSWORD is disabled.

services.name: picoshare
services.description: Minimalist self-hosted file-sharing service for generating shareable download links to files of any size.
services.installation: PicoShare stores everything in a single SQLite database under /data (no external datastore add-on needed). REQUIRED: set PS_SHARED_SECRET to a strong random value before exposing publicly (it is the admin/upload passphrase) and rotate the default. Optional: enable Litestream streaming backups by setting LITESTREAM_BUCKET / LITESTREAM_ENDPOINT / LITESTREAM_ACCESS_KEY_ID / LITESTREAM_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY (requires an external S3-compatible bucket; omitted from addons since Kubero add-ons do not cover S3). The persistent volume at /data must be retained across upgrades or all uploads and links are lost.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: pixelfed
services.description: Free, federated (ActivityPub) photo-sharing platform - a self-hosted, ethical alternative to Instagram for the Fediverse.
services.installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Pixelfed web/php-fpm container only. The zknt/pixelfed image serves over HTTP on port 80 and ships a php-fpm + nginx supervisord bundle. REQUIRED first-run steps: 1) Generate an APP_KEY with 'php artisan key:generate' (or 'openssl rand -base64 32') and set it as 'base64:...' - rotate the placeholder before going live. 2) After the DB is up, exec into the pod and run 'php artisan migrate --force' and 'php artisan storage:link', then 'php artisan import:cities' and 'php artisan instance:actor' to seed the federation actor. 3) Create the first admin with 'php artisan user:create' (or register then 'php artisan user:admin <id>'). COMPANION SERVICES to deploy separately for a full instance (same zknt/pixelfed image, different command): a HORIZON QUEUE WORKER ('php artisan horizon') is MANDATORY for federation, media optimization, and notifications - deploy it as a second app pointing at the same MariaDB + Valkey; an optional SCHEDULER/cron loop ('php artisan schedule:work') for digests and cleanup. Pixelfed stores uploads/avatars under /var/www/storage (mounted here) - the worker pod must share or replicate this path (use RWX storage) so federated media is reachable. Object storage: for production at scale point FILESYSTEM_DRIVER + AWS_* / S3 vars at an external S3 bucket (e.g. the RustFS add-on) instead of the local volume; omit it here for a single-node start. Rotate the MariaDB password, Valkey is password-less in-cluster. Set ADMIN_DOMAIN / APP_DOMAIN to your real host before federating - it is baked into ActivityPub actor URLs and cannot be changed cleanly later.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: pleroma
services.description: Lightweight, federated (ActivityPub) social networking server - a self-hosted, resource-friendly microblogging alternative for the Fediverse.
services.installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Pleroma server container (the BEAM/Elixir OTP release on git.pleroma.social/pleroma/pleroma:stable, HTTP on port 4000). Pleroma is single-process: the web endpoint AND the Oban background job queues (federation delivery, scheduled tasks, media processing) all run inside this one container, so NO separate worker/scheduler companion is needed. Pleroma uses PostgreSQL ONLY - it does NOT use Redis/Valkey. REQUIRED before first boot: 1) Rotate SECRET_KEY_BASE - generate with 'openssl rand -base64 64' (or 'mix phx.gen.secret') and set it; the placeholder MUST be replaced. 2) The Postgres add-on database needs the citext, pg_trgm and uuid-ossp extensions - exec into the pleroma pod and run the DB migrations, then enable them: connect to the db and run 'CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS citext; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";' (the official entrypoint runs './bin/pleroma_ctl migrate' on start). 3) Create the first admin AFTER migrations: exec in and run './bin/pleroma_ctl user new <nickname> <email> --admin'. CONFIG: this image is driven by an Elixir config file at PLEROMA_CONFIG_PATH (/etc/pleroma/config.exs); the official docker entrypoint generates it from the DOMAIN / INSTANCE_NAME / ADMIN_EMAIL / NOTIFY_EMAIL / DB_* / SECRET_KEY_BASE env vars set here on first run - mount the /etc/pleroma volume so the generated config persists. DOMAIN is baked into ActivityPub actor URLs and cannot be changed cleanly after federating, so set it to your real host before going live. COMPANION SERVICES: none are mandatory in-cluster (Kubero ingress provides the TLS reverse proxy that a bare-metal install would need from nginx, and handles the optional MediaProxy caching). For a production frontend you may later swap the bundled Pleroma-FE for Soapbox/Mangane by uploading it to the /var/lib/pleroma/static volume. For object storage at scale, point Pleroma's Uploaders.S3 config (AWS_* / S3 settings in config.exs) at an external S3 bucket (e.g. the RustFS add-on) instead of the local uploads volume; omit it here for a single-node start.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: posthog
services.description: Open-source product analytics, session replay, feature flags, A/B testing and surveys platform built on PostgreSQL, ClickHouse and Kafka.
services.installation:
PostHog is a LARGE multi-service stack; this template runs the PRIMARY all-in-one image posthog/posthog (the `web` role: Django backend + frontend) on HTTP port 8000. It is NOT fully functional alone - PostHog needs several companion services and TWO datastores that are NOT in the allowed add-on set (Kafka and S3-compatible object storage), so plan to deploy those alongside it.

ADD-ONS provisioned here: postgres (relational app DB), clickhouse (the OLAP event store - the heart of PostHog analytics, reached at clickhouse-posthog-clickhouse:8123 over plain HTTP, so CLICKHOUSE_SECURE=false and CLICKHOUSE_VERIFY=false), and valkey (Redis-compatible cache/broker at rfr-posthog-valkey-readwrite:6379, no password).

NOT provisionable as add-ons - deploy these as separate Kubero apps and update the *_HOSTS/endpoint env vars to point at them:
  - KAFKA / event bus: PostHog requires Kafka. Use Redpanda (image `redpandadata/redpanda:latest`, Kafka API on 9092) or `bitnami/kafka`. Set KAFKA_HOSTS on EVERY PostHog container to host:9092.
  - OBJECT STORAGE (session recordings, exports, uploads): PostHog requires S3-compatible storage. Deploy the platform's RustFS/S3 add-on or `minio/minio`, then set OBJECT_STORAGE_ENABLED=true, OBJECT_STORAGE_ENDPOINT, OBJECT_STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY_ID, OBJECT_STORAGE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, OBJECT_STORAGE_BUCKET.
  - TEMPORAL (batch exports / scheduled jobs): image `temporalio/auto-setup` on gRPC 7233; only needed if you use batch exports.

COMPANION POSTHOG CONTAINERS (same posthog/posthog image, different command - deploy each as its own Kubero app sharing the SAME env vars / DATABASE_URL / CLICKHOUSE / KAFKA / REDIS):
  - WORKER (Celery): command `./bin/docker-worker-celery --with-scheduler` - runs async tasks AND the scheduler; REQUIRED for the app to work.
  - PLUGIN-SERVER / ingestion: command `./bin/plugin-server` - consumes Kafka events into ClickHouse; REQUIRED for any analytics data to appear.
  - MIGRATE (one-shot, run FIRST): command `python manage.py migrate && python manage.py migrate_clickhouse` to create Postgres + ClickHouse schemas before the web/worker start. The migrate-clickhouse step needs Kafka reachable.
  - (Optional high-scale, separate Rust images) CAPTURE -> `ghcr.io/posthog/posthog/capture:latest` (event intake on Kafka), FEATURE-FLAGS -> `ghcr.io/posthog/posthog/feature-flags:latest`.

ROTATE before production: SECRET_KEY and ENCRYPTION_SALT_KEYS (both set here to placeholder `replace-with-openssl-rand-hex-32` values - generate real ones with `openssl rand -hex 32`; ENCRYPTION_SALT_KEYS must be a 32-byte base64/urlsafe key and CANNOT change after first boot or stored secrets break), plus the postgres and clickhouse add-on passwords (default `posthog`).

FIRST-RUN: run the MIGRATE job, then start web + worker + plugin-server; open {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} and complete the on-screen setup wizard to create the first organization and admin user. SITE_URL is preset to your public URL and IS_BEHIND_PROXY=true / DISABLE_SECURE_SSL_REDIRECT=true are set because TLS is terminated at the Kubero ingress. NOTE: PostHog deprecated its official Helm chart - self-hosting is officially Docker-Compose-based and unsupported for high scale; PostHog Cloud is the recommended path for large workloads.

services.name: prestashop
services.description: Open-source e-commerce platform for building and managing online stores, with a built-in admin back office and a marketplace of modules and themes.
services.installation: PrestaShop requires MySQL/MariaDB (it does not support PostgreSQL). This template provisions the prestashop-mysql add-on (db/user/password all 'prestashop' - ROTATE before production). PS_INSTALL_AUTO=1 runs the installer automatically on first boot using ADMIN_MAIL/ADMIN_PASSWD - change ADMIN_PASSWD (default 'prestashop' here) and ADMIN_MAIL immediately, then delete or rename the /admin folder after install as PrestaShop requires. PS_DOMAIN is set to the app host so install URLs resolve; if you later change the domain, update Shop URL under Shop Parameters > Traffic & SEO. Optional: set PS_ENABLE_SSL=1 once TLS is fronting the app.

services.name: pretix
services.description: Open-source event ticketing and registration platform for selling tickets to conferences, festivals and exhibitions (Eventbrite alternative).
services.installation: Pretix is a Django stack. This template runs the PRIMARY web container (image pretix/standalone with command 'web', listening on container port 80 - nginx+gunicorn under supervisord), wired to the Postgres and Valkey/Redis add-ons provisioned here. The image is configured via PRETIX_<SECTION>_<KEY> environment variables (the env-var equivalent of /etc/pretix/pretix.cfg). You MUST deploy the companion services as separate apps, all sharing the SAME Postgres, Redis and env keys below: (1) Task worker - image pretix/standalone with command 'taskworker' (Celery; REQUIRED - sending emails, generating exports/invoices and all async jobs fail without it); (2) Cron/beat - image pretix/standalone with command 'cron' (periodic/scheduled tasks). Set PRETIX_PRETIX_URL to this app's public URL (token below). ROTATE before exposing: PRETIX_DJANGO_SECRET (via 'openssl rand -hex 50'), the Postgres add-on password, and configure real SMTP (PRETIX_MAIL_* are placeholders - pretix cannot send tickets without working email). First run: the web container applies DB migrations on boot; create the initial admin with 'pretix createsuperuser' (or set PRETIX_PRETIXD_* and use the web onboarding). The /data volume holds uploaded media, the data secret and the cache - mount the SAME volume (RWX) on the worker if you generate exports there. Redis here is used for cache, sessions and as the Celery broker. Docs: https://docs.pretix.eu/self-hosting/installation/docker_smallscale/

services.name: pydio
services.description: Self-hosted document sharing and collaboration platform: a file-sync, sharing and content-management server for organizations.
services.installation: Pydio Cells requires a MySQL/MariaDB database (provided here by the mariadb add-on). Rotate the default DB password ('pydio') and the random CELLS_ENABLE_METRICS/secret placeholders before going to production. On first run Cells auto-installs against the database when CELLS_INSTALL_YAML is unset and the DB env vars are present; otherwise open the app URL and complete the web installer using DB host pydio-mysql:3306, db/user/pass = pydio. Set CELLS_SITE_EXTERNAL to the app URL (handled via {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}). TLS is terminated at the ingress, so the container runs plain HTTP (CELLS_SITE_NO_TLS=1). Cells stores config, logs and uploaded files under /var/cells (persistent volume). For large-scale search/object storage Cells embeds MinIO and a Bleve index by default; no external Elasticsearch/S3 is required for a single-node deployment.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: redash
services.description: Open-source data visualization and dashboarding tool to query, explore, and share insights from 30+ SQL/NoSQL/API data sources.
services.installation: Redash is a multi-process stack: every service runs the SAME redash/redash image with a different command. This template runs the primary web server (command `server`, port 5000). You MUST ALSO deploy these companion deployments from the identical redash/redash:10.1.0 image, same env vars, to make it functional: a Celery WORKER (`celery worker --app=redash.worker --queue=queries,celery`) for running queries, a SCHEDULER (`celery worker --app=redash.worker --beat --queue=periodic,emails,schemas`) for refreshes and alerts, and a one-time INIT/migration job (`create_db`) that must run ONCE before first boot to create the schema. Bundled add-ons Postgres and Redis (provided by the Valkey add-on) are provisioned here. ROTATE before exposing: REDASH_COOKIE_SECRET and REDASH_SECRET_KEY (each `openssl rand -hex 32`; SECRET_KEY must be 32+ chars and must NOT change after data exists or saved credentials become undecryptable), plus the Postgres add-on password. Set REDASH_HOST to your public URL. After the create_db job completes and the server is up, open the app and the first registration becomes the admin. Optional: outbound email needs REDASH_MAIL_* SMTP env vars (not provisioned).

services.name: revolt
services.description: Open-source, self-hostable Discord alternative: a real-time chat platform with servers, channels, DMs, voice and file sharing (now branded Stoat).
services.installation: PLATFORM NOTE: Revolt requires real MongoDB features (capped collections, collation indexes) that Stackblaze DocumentDB and FerretDB do not fully provide — this template is marked unsupported until native MongoDB is available; API-only deploy may still fail at DB init. This template runs ONLY the PRIMARY Revolt backend API (the Rust 'delta' monolith, image ghcr.io/stoatchat/api, HTTP port 8000). Revolt (recently rebranded 'Stoat') is a MULTI-CONTAINER microservice stack; the API alone is not a usable deployment - you MUST deploy the companion services below pointing at the SAME datastores, and supply a shared Revolt.toml config file. CONFIG: Revolt is configured PRIMARILY via a Revolt.toml file bind-mounted at /Revolt.toml (mounted here at the persistent /config volume - drop your Revolt.toml there or mount it at the root); the env vars below override the database/redis/rabbit connection URLs that Revolt.toml otherwise carries. In Revolt.toml set hosts.app to your public https URL ({{KUBERO_APP_URL}}), generate the auth/file/voice secrets, and configure the S3/MinIO file backend. ROTATE: the FerretDB postgresql.auth password, and ALL secrets in Revolt.toml (api keys, auth pepper, file-server token, vapid/push keys, livekit keys) - the defaults are placeholders. ADD-ONS PROVISIONED HERE: ferretdb (MongoDB-wire-compatible - Revolt's primary datastore; prefer external real MongoDB if available), valkey (Redis/KeyDB-compatible cache + pub/sub), and rabbitmq (push-notification + cross-service message queue). EXTERNAL SERVICE NOT PROVISIONED: Revolt requires an S3-compatible object store for ALL uploaded files/avatars/attachments (upstream ships MinIO image minio/minio) - deploy the RustFS/MinIO S3 add-on and point autumn at it via Revolt.toml [files] s3 settings; the local /config volume does NOT hold attachments. COMPANION SERVICES to deploy (all ghcr.io/stoatchat, pin to the same tag e.g. v0.13.6, all share Revolt.toml + the same Mongo/Redis/Rabbit/S3): events (websocket gateway, ghcr.io/stoatchat/events), autumn (file server, ghcr.io/stoatchat/file-server), january (link/media proxy, ghcr.io/stoatchat/proxy), crond (scheduled tasks, ghcr.io/stoatchat/crond), pushd (push-notification worker / rabbit consumer, ghcr.io/stoatchat/pushd), web (frontend client, ghcr.io/stoatchat/for-web), and a reverse proxy (caddy) to route the public domain to web/api/events/autumn. Voice is optional via voice-ingress + livekit (ghcr.io/stoatchat/livekit-server). FIRST-RUN: bring up Mongo/Redis/Rabbit and S3, then the api + companion services; open the web client, create the first account, and configure the instance.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: rustdesk
services.description: Self-hosted RustDesk relay/rendezvous server (hbbs + hbbr) for running your own private, open-source remote desktop infrastructure.
services.installation: The rustdesk-server-s6 image runs both hbbs (ID/rendezvous) and hbbr (relay) in one container via s6-overlay. On first start it generates an ECDSA key pair in /data (id_ed25519 / id_ed25519.pub); copy the public key from /data/id_ed25519.pub and distribute it to RustDesk clients as the Key, with this host as the ID/Relay server. Set RELAY to this app's public host so clients can reach the relay. Native RustDesk clients connect over TCP/UDP 21115-21119; those are NOT proxied by the HTTP ingress and require LoadBalancer/NodePort exposure of those ports. Container port 21118 is the hbbs web/websocket port. ENCRYPTED_ONLY=1 rejects unencrypted clients. Persist /data to keep the key pair and the SQLite db_v2.sqlite3 across restarts.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: rustfs
services.description: High-performance, S3-compatible distributed object storage written in Rust, with a built-in web console; a drop-in MinIO/Ceph alternative.
services.installation: RustFS is itself the datastore: it persists all objects and metadata on disk under /data using erasure coding, so no database add-on is required. The S3-compatible API and web console are both served on container port 9000 (console reachable at /rustfs/console). Set RUSTFS_VOLUMES to the data path. IMPORTANT: rotate RUSTFS_ACCESS_KEY and RUSTFS_SECRET_KEY before exposing the endpoint - the template defaults (rustfsadmin / replace-with-openssl-rand-hex-32) are root credentials. After first boot, log in to the console with those credentials, then create scoped access keys and buckets for your apps. For multi-node erasure-set durability, run several replicas and point RUSTFS_VOLUMES at multiple mounts; this single-node template gives one volume suitable for dev/small workloads. No external S3, search engine, or SMTP service is needed.

services.name: saleor
services.description: GraphQL-first, headless open-source e-commerce platform for building scalable storefronts, marketplaces and digital commerce.
services.installation: Saleor is a multi-container stack. This template deploys the PRIMARY GraphQL API/server image (ghcr.io/saleor/saleor). After first boot, exec into the pod and run migrations + create an admin: `python manage.py migrate` then `python manage.py createsuperuser` (or set the pre-baked superuser via env). You MUST also deploy these companion services, all using the SAME ghcr.io/saleor/saleor image with the same DATABASE_URL/REDIS_URL/SECRET_KEY env: (1) Celery WORKER -> command `celery --app saleor.celeryconf:app worker --loglevel=info`; (2) Celery BEAT SCHEDULER -> command `celery --app saleor.celeryconf:app beat --scheduler saleor.schedulers.schedulers.DatabaseScheduler`. The admin DASHBOARD is a separate static SPA image `ghcr.io/saleor/saleor-dashboard` (nginx, port 80) pointed at this API's /graphql/ endpoint. Add-ons: a Postgres add-on (Saleor recommends PostgreSQL) and a Valkey/Redis add-on (used as Celery broker + cache) are provisioned. Rotate the default Postgres password and the SECRET_KEY (replace the placeholder with `openssl rand -hex 32`). Set ALLOWED_HOSTS/ALLOWED_GRAPHQL_ORIGINS to your real host. For media/static uploads Saleor recommends external S3-compatible object storage in production; this template mounts a local volume at /app/media for default local file storage. A full-text search backend is optional and not included.

services.name: saltcorn
services.description: Free and open-source no-code application builder for creating database-backed web apps without writing code.
services.installation: Image: saltcorn/saltcorn (official). Saltcorn requires PostgreSQL; it connects via the discrete PG* vars pointing at the bundled postgres add-on (host saltcorn-postgresql-rw). Rotate SALTCORN_SESSION_SECRET (any long random string, e.g. `openssl rand -hex 32`) and the Postgres password (default 'saltcorn') before exposing publicly. On first run Saltcorn auto-initializes the schema, then presents a setup wizard to create the admin user and site name - complete this immediately. User-installed plugins, uploaded files and config persist on the /saltcorn volume. Multi-tenant mode is off by default (SALTCORN_MULTI_TENANT=false).

services.name: searxng
services.description: Privacy-respecting, hackable open-source metasearch engine that aggregates results from 70+ search services without tracking or profiling users.
services.installation: SearXNG is a stateless metasearch engine: it stores no user search history and needs no SQL database. It does use the bundled Valkey (Redis-compatible) add-on for the optional limiter/bot-detection and caching, wired in via the SEARXNG_REDIS_URL env var (passwordless in-cluster). IMPORTANT: rotate SEARXNG_SECRET to a unique random value before going live (e.g. `openssl rand -hex 32`); the placeholder here must not be used in production. SEARXNG_BASE_URL is auto-filled with this app's public URL so generated links and OpenSearch descriptions are correct. Mount /etc/searxng to persist settings.yml (engines, theme, limiter, UI), which the image generates on first start; edit it there to enable/disable search engines, turn on the limiter (limiter.toml), or restrict instance access. No SMTP, S3, or external Elasticsearch is required.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: seaweedfs
services.description: Fast distributed storage system for blobs, objects, files and a data lake, with an S3-compatible API and a filer web UI.
services.installation: Runs as an all-in-one node via the `server` command (master + volume + filer), with the S3 gateway enabled. The filer web UI is on container port 8888; the S3-compatible API is on port 8333 and the master UI on 9333 (expose internally as needed). All metadata and data persist under /data (default leveldb filer store) so no external database add-on is required. For production scale, split master/volume/filer into separate deployments and point the filer at an external metadata store. There is no built-in auth on the filer UI by default; place it behind the ingress/auth layer and configure S3 access keys before exposing the S3 endpoint.

services.name: sharkey
services.description: Feature-rich, ActivityPub-federated microblogging server - a Misskey fork for the Fediverse with post editing and the Mastodon API.
services.installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Sharkey Node.js web/server container only (official image joinsharkey/sharkey, listens on HTTP port 3000). CONFIG: Sharkey (like its Misskey parent) is configured PRIMARILY via .config/default.yml, not env vars alone, so BEFORE first boot you MUST drop a default.yml into the mounted /sharkey/.config volume - set 'url' to your public https URL (it is baked into ActivityPub actor URLs and cannot be changed cleanly later; {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} is provided below for reference), and set db.host=sharkey-postgresql-rw / db.port=5432 / db.db=sharkey / db.user=sharkey / db.pass=sharkey and redis.host=rfr-sharkey-valkey-readwrite / redis.port=6379. The env vars below (POSTGRES_*/DATABASE_*/REDIS_*) mirror those values for the parts Sharkey reads from the environment; keep them in sync with default.yml. FIRST-RUN: the official image runs DB migrations automatically on start (pnpm migrate); once the pod is healthy open the site, create the first account (it becomes the instance admin), then configure the instance in the admin control panel. ROTATE the Postgres password (default 'sharkey') in BOTH the postgres add-on and default.yml to match. ADD-ONS PROVISIONED: PostgreSQL (primary datastore) and Valkey/Redis (queue + cache, password-less in-cluster). COMPANION SERVICES: Sharkey's background job queue (federation delivery/inbox, media processing, notifications) runs IN-PROCESS in this same image by default, so NO separate worker container is required for a single-node start; for scale you can run additional replicas of joinsharkey/sharkey with MK_ONLY_SERVER / MK_ONLY_QUEUE (clusterLimits in default.yml) pointing at the same Postgres + Valkey. EXTERNAL SERVICES not provisioned here: object storage (S3-compatible, e.g. the RustFS add-on) is strongly recommended for media at scale - configure under 'objectStorage' in default.yml instead of relying on the local /sharkey/files volume; SMTP for email verification; and an OPTIONAL MeiliSearch backend (image getmeili/meilisearch) for full-text post search, configured under 'meilisearch' in default.yml.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: shopware
services.description: Open-source headless commerce platform for building online stores, marketplaces, and B2B/D2C experiences with Symfony and Vue.
services.installation: Image: dockware/play (the de-facto managed Shopware 6 Docker image; runs Apache + PHP-FPM on port 80). Shopware REQUIRES MySQL 8 / MariaDB 10.11+ (NOT Postgres) - the mariadb add-on is wired in. Valkey (Redis-compatible) is wired in for sessions and app cache. First-run: the image ships a pre-installed demo shop using its bundled MySQL; for production point DATABASE_URL at the external mariadb add-on (set INSTALL=1 / run `bin/console system:install` on first boot to migrate the schema onto it), then create an admin via `bin/console user:create`. Default admin login of the bundled demo is admin / shopware - ROTATE immediately. Rotate APP_SECRET and INSTANCE_ID_HASH (placeholders below) with `openssl rand -hex 32` and the mariadb/db passwords. COMPANION SERVICES (deploy as separate apps sharing the same DATABASE_URL/Redis): a message-consumer worker (`bin/console messenger:consume async,low_priority`, same dockware/play image, run as a long-lived command), a scheduled-task worker (`bin/console scheduled-task:run`), and a cron/cache-warmer. SEARCH: Shopware scales poorly past ~50k products on MySQL search - deploy OpenSearch 2.x separately (NOT an available operator add-on; run it standalone) and set OPENSEARCH_URL + SHOPWARE_ES_ENABLED=1 + SHOPWARE_ES_INDEXING_ENABLED=1. Optionally front the storefront with Varnish/HTTP cache for production.

services.name: snipe-it
services.description: Free, open-source IT asset and license management system for tracking hardware, software licenses, accessories, and consumables.
services.installation: Snipe-IT is a Laravel app backed by MariaDB/MySQL (it does NOT support Postgres). It requires a 32-char APP_KEY: rotate APP_KEY to a value generated with 'php artisan key:generate --show' (or 'openssl rand -base64 32' formatted as base64:...) before going live, and rotate the MariaDB password. Valkey is wired for cache/session/queue drivers. On first run the app auto-runs migrations; then open the URL and complete the web-based pre-flight setup wizard to create the admin user. Mail (MAIL_*) is optional but needed for notifications.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: suitecrm
services.description: Open-source customer relationship management for sales, marketing and service teams: accounts, contacts, leads, opportunities, cases and workflows.
services.installation: Runs the Bitnami SuiteCRM image (bitnami/suitecrm). SuiteCRM only supports MariaDB/MySQL (no PostgreSQL) — this template ships the bundled mariadb addon (default db/user/password 'suitecrm', rotate before production). On first boot the admin account is provisioned from SUITECRM_USERNAME / SUITECRM_PASSWORD / SUITECRM_EMAIL — rotate SUITECRM_PASSWORD (placeholder value) and the mariadb password to match SUITECRM_DATABASE_PASSWORD. The valkey addon is wired as the optional Redis cache/session store via SUITECRM_REDIS_HOST (Valkey is Redis-compatible and password-less here); remove it if not wanted. SUITECRM_HOST is set from the app URL token so generated links resolve. Persistent app data (uploads, custom modules, config) lives at /bitnami/suitecrm — back this up. For outbound email configure SMTP from the SuiteCRM admin UI after install. Scheduler/cron jobs should be run via an external CronJob hitting cron.php.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: taiga
services.description: Open-source agile project management platform with Scrum and Kanban boards, backlogs, sprints, and issue tracking.
services.installation: This template deploys taiga-back (the Django API server) ONLY. Taiga is a multi-container stack and is NOT functional without its companion services, which must be deployed separately as their own apps/sidecars: (1) taiga-async = same image taigaio/taiga-back:latest with command celery worker (the background task/Celery worker, shares POSTGRES_* + RABBITMQ + TAIGA_SECRET_KEY); (2) taiga-front = taigaio/taiga-front:latest (the web UI, served on port 80); (3) taiga-events = taigaio/taiga-events:latest (websocket push server, needs its own RabbitMQ broker + the same TAIGA_SECRET_KEY); (4) taiga-protected = taigaio/taiga-protected:latest (signed-URL attachment proxy, needs TAIGA_SECRET_KEY); (5) taiga-gateway = nginx:1.19-alpine routing front/back/events/protected on port 9000 (the real public entrypoint). This template provisions the shared postgres and rabbitmq add-ons that taiga-back, taiga-async, and taiga-events all connect to. ROTATE: TAIGA_SECRET_KEY (set to replace-with-openssl-rand-hex-32 here; generate with `openssl rand -hex 32` and use the SAME value across back/async/events/protected), the postgres password, and the RABBITMQ_PASS. FIRST RUN: exec into taiga-back and run `python manage.py migrate` then `python manage.py loaddata initial_project_templates` and create a superuser with `python manage.py createsuperuser`. Note EVENTS_PUSH_BACKEND_URL and CELERY_BROKER_URL point at the rabbitmq add-on (amqp://taiga:taiga@taiga-rabbitmq:5672).
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: temporal
services.description: Durable execution platform that runs reliable, fault-tolerant workflows and activities as code at scale.
services.installation: This template deploys the PRIMARY Temporal server using the all-in-one `temporalio/auto-setup` image. On first boot auto-setup creates the database schema and starts all four server roles (frontend, history, matching, internal worker) in a single pod. PORTS: the server speaks gRPC on 7233 (the address SDK clients/workers connect to) and exposes an HTTP API on 7243 (the containerPort published here). Add-on: a Postgres add-on backs both the default and visibility keyspaces (DBNAME=temporal, VISIBILITY_DBNAME=temporal_visibility, auto-created by auto-setup). NOTE: auto-setup is the convenience bundle for self-hosting; Temporal recommends the separate `temporalio/server` + `temporalio/admin-tools` images with externally-managed schema for large production deployments, and advanced/large visibility setups can additionally use Elasticsearch or OpenSearch (set ENABLE_ES=true + ES_SEEDS) which is NOT provisioned here. COMPANION SERVICES to deploy as their own Kubero apps: (1) WEB UI -> image `temporalio/ui:latest` (listens on HTTP 8080), set TEMPORAL_ADDRESS=temporal-<pod/service>:7233 and TEMPORAL_CORS_ORIGINS={{KUBERO_APP_URL}}; this is the human-facing dashboard. (2) ADMIN/CLI -> image `temporalio/admin-tools:latest` for running `tctl`/`temporal` operator commands and manual schema upgrades. (3) Your own WORKERS are separate apps built from your code with the Temporal SDK, pointed at this server's 7233 gRPC endpoint. ROTATE the default Postgres password before going live. Set TEMPORAL_ADDRESS/namespaces via tctl after boot; a `default` namespace is auto-registered.

services.name: totaljs-flow
services.description: Open-source visual flow-based programming editor for building real-time data-processing and integration pipelines with drag-and-drop components.
services.installation: Image: totalplatform/flow (official Total.js platform image). Total.js Flow is fully file-based - it needs NO external database; all flow designs, custom components, uploaded files and config persist on the /www/flow volume, so back that up. The app listens on port 8000. The editor is unauthenticated by default: set FLOW_TOKEN to a long random value (e.g. `openssl rand -hex 32`) to require a token, and place it behind the ingress TLS/auth as appropriate before exposing publicly - rotate FLOW_TOKEN from the placeholder. FLOW_NAME just labels the instance. On first run the empty flow workspace loads at the root URL; create or import flows from the designer. If you later need a message queue or datastore, deploy it separately and reference it from inside your flows (Flow has its own component nodes for Postgres, MongoDB, Redis, MQTT, etc.).

services.name: tracardi
services.description: Open-source API-first Customer Data Platform (CDP) for collecting events, building profiles and segments, and orchestrating real-time customer journeys.
services.installation: Tracardi is a multi-container stack; this template runs ONLY the primary API/server image tracardi/tracardi-api on internal port 80. It REQUIRES an external Elasticsearch (7.x) or OpenSearch cluster as its primary datastore for all events/profiles/segments - Elasticsearch is NOT one of the supported operator add-ons, so deploy it separately (e.g. docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:7.17.x) and point ELASTIC_HOST at it (default here http://elasticsearch:9200) before Tracardi will start. The MySQL config DB is provisioned via the mariadb add-on and Redis cache via the valkey add-on. Deploy these COMPANION services from the same Tracardi image family, sharing the SAME ELASTIC_HOST / REDIS_HOST / MYSQL_HOST env values: tracardi/tracardi-gui (the web admin GUI, internal port 80 - expose it with its own Service/Ingress, this is what operators log into), tracardi/update-worker (background workflow/segmentation worker), and tracardi/apm (auto profile-merging / background processing worker). Set PRODUCTION=yes and DEMO=no for a clean install. ROTATE before exposing: the mariadb (MYSQL) and any admin passwords; the default admin login is created on first boot (admin@tracardi.com) - change it immediately. AUTOLOAD_BRIDGE/DEMO data and the default credentials are for evaluation only.

services.name: traduora
services.description: Open-source translation management platform (TMS) for teams to collaboratively manage app localization, with a REST API, CLI, and import/export.
services.installation: Single all-in-one everco/ever-traduora image (API + web UI) on port 8080, backed by the bundled PostgreSQL add-on (TR_DB_TYPE=postgres; Traduora supports both MySQL and Postgres and we pick Postgres per convention). ROTATE before production: TR_SECRET (used to sign/verify auth tokens; generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`) and the Postgres add-on password. Database tables are created/migrated automatically on first boot. TR_SIGNUPS_ENABLED is left true so you can register the first account from the UI, which becomes the org owner; set it to false afterwards to lock down public signups. Set TR_VIRTUAL_HOST to the deployed host so URLs in invitation/password emails resolve correctly. Outbound email (TR_MAIL_*) and any S3 export targets are NOT provisioned here and must be configured separately if needed.
services.license: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

services.name: trudesk
services.description: Open-source helpdesk and ticketing system with email piping, knowledge base, live notifications, and per-team agent workflows.
services.installation: Trudesk is a Node.js helpdesk that stores all data in MongoDB; the documentdb add-on below provides the MongoDB-compatible backend. The TD_MONGODB_* vars point at the in-cluster documentdb service (host documentdb-service-trudesk-documentdb, port 10260) using SCRAM-SHA-256 over TLS, user 'mongoadmin'. Because the endpoint uses a self-signed cert, TD_MONGODB_AUTHENTICATION_DATABASE is set to admin and the connection allows invalid TLS certs. Rotate the documentdb password from the default 'trudesk' before exposing publicly. On first load open {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}/install to run the web setup wizard, which creates the database schema and your super-admin account. Trudesk runs its own internal Redis for caching/sessions inside the container, so no external Redis add-on is needed. Configure inbound (IMAP mailbox) and outbound (SMTP) email in the admin panel after install to enable ticket creation from email. Uploads and generated assets are persisted on the mounted volume.

services.name: unibee
services.description: Open-source, gateway-agnostic subscription billing and invoicing platform for SaaS businesses.
services.installation: Primary web/API container only (unibee/api). DATABASE_LINK uses a MySQL DSN, so this template wires the mariadb add-on (UniBee speaks MySQL, not postgres). The valkey add-on has no password, so REDIS_PASSWORD is left empty. UniBee is a multi-container stack: also deploy the companion services as separate Kubero apps and route them behind one ingress (UniBee normally fronts them with nginx): admin merchant portal = unibee/admin-portal:v1.9.0 (port 80), user portal = unibee/user-portal:v1.9.0 (port 80), and the licensing service = unibee/license-api:v1.0.2-premium (port 80). Set UNIBEE_API_URL and the portal URLs to your public host once routing is set up; replace UNIBEE_LICENSE with your key (blank works in stand-alone MODE for the open-source edition). Rotate the mariadb password from the slug default. First run: open the admin portal and create the first merchant/admin account.

services.name: unomi
services.description: Open-source Java/OSGi Customer Data Platform that manages profiles, segments and events with a privacy-first context server and standards-based API.
services.installation: Apache Unomi REQUIRES an external Elasticsearch (1.x/2.x stream) or OpenSearch cluster for all profile/event/segment storage; it is NOT one of the supported operator add-ons, so deploy Elasticsearch/OpenSearch separately and point UNOMI_ELASTICSEARCH_ADDRESSES (or UNOMI_OPENSEARCH_ADDRESSES) at it before Unomi will start. Unomi's own Karaf runtime state and config live on the persistent volume at /opt/apache-unomi/data, so no SQL/NoSQL operator add-on is provisioned here. The HTTP context-server and REST API listen on 8181 (a TLS port 9443 and Karaf SSH 8102 are also exposed by the image but are not the HTTP container port and need their own Service/Ingress). UNOMI_THIRDPARTY_PROVIDER1_KEY is the shared secret used by trusted server-side event collectors and MUST be rotated from the template placeholder before production; UNOMI_THIRDPARTY_PROVIDER1_IPADDRESSES whitelists which source IPs may send protected events (here left open as 0.0.0.0/0 for first boot - tighten it). After boot, manage profiles and rules via the REST API / Karaf console; there is no bundled web admin UI.

services.name: uvdesk
services.description: Open-source PHP/Symfony helpdesk and support ticketing system with email piping, knowledgebase, and multi-channel agent workflows.
services.installation: UVdesk is wizard-driven: on first load complete the web installer at {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}/install. When prompted for the database, use host 'uvdesk-mysql', port 3306, database/user/password 'uvdesk' (matching the mariadb add-on below) and create your super-admin account there. There is no separate root step needed for an external DB. UVdesk requires MySQL/MariaDB only (no PostgreSQL support). Outbound/inbound email (SMTP + IMAP mailbox) must be configured in the admin panel after install for ticket creation from email. Rotate the mariadb password from the default 'uvdesk' before exposing publicly. No official Docker image is published by the project; this uses the actively maintained community image dietermartens/uvdesk.

services.name: wger
services.description: Self-hosted FLOSS fitness and workout manager that lets you track workouts, nutrition, body weight, and exercises.
services.installation: Single-container Django app served via gunicorn on port 8000. Rotate SECRET_KEY and SIGNING_KEY (each a unique 50-char random string; they MUST differ) and the postgres password before going to production. Companion services deployed as add-ons: PostgreSQL (primary DB) and a Valkey (Redis-protocol) instance used for both the Django cache and the Celery broker/backend. Migrations run automatically on boot (DJANGO_PERFORM_MIGRATIONS=True). On first run an admin user 'admin' (password 'adminadmin') is created via DJANGO_DB_BOOTSTRAP_USER defaults; change it immediately. Set CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS / SITE_URL to your real https URL (templated below). Uploaded media and static files persist on the mounted volume. Note: the periodic exercise/ingredient sync tasks run via Celery against the Valkey add-on; no separate worker pod is templated, so heavy background sync may be limited.

services.name: woocommerce
services.description: The world's most popular open-source e-commerce platform, built as a WordPress plugin to turn any WordPress site into a fully featured online store.
services.installation: WooCommerce is a WordPress plugin, so this template runs the official wordpress image (PHP/Apache) and you install WooCommerce from inside wp-admin. WordPress requires MySQL/MariaDB (it does not support PostgreSQL), so the mariadb add-on is shipped. First run: complete the WordPress install wizard, then in wp-admin go to Plugins > Add New, search 'WooCommerce', install + activate, and run the WooCommerce setup wizard (store address, currency, payment, shipping). Rotate the MariaDB password and all eight WORDPRESS_AUTH_KEY/SALT secrets (openssl rand -base64 64) before production. WP_HOME/WP_SITEURL are pinned to the app URL so checkout and admin URLs resolve. Persistent data (wp-content: plugins incl. WooCommerce, themes, uploads/product images) lives on the mounted volume at /var/www/html. For email (order receipts) configure an SMTP plugin; for payments add a gateway plugin (Stripe/PayPal).

services.name: wordpress-multisite
services.description: The world's most popular open-source CMS, preconfigured for Multisite to run a network of sites from a single WordPress install.
services.installation: WordPress requires MySQL/MariaDB (it does not support PostgreSQL), so this template ships the mariadb add-on. Multisite is enabled at the WordPress config layer via WORDPRESS_CONFIG_EXTRA (WP_ALLOW_MULTISITE=true). After the first-run install wizard completes, go to Tools > Network Setup in wp-admin to create the network, then add the printed wp-config.php / .htaccess rules. Subdomain multisite needs a wildcard DNS record (*.{{KUBERO_APP_HOST}}) and matching ingress/TLS; subdirectory multisite needs none. Rotate WORDPRESS_AUTH_KEY/SECURE_AUTH_KEY/LOGGED_IN_KEY/NONCE_KEY and the four matching salts (openssl rand -base64 64) plus the MariaDB password before going to production. Persistent data (wp-content: uploads, plugins, themes, languages) lives on the mounted volume at /var/www/html.

services.name: xwiki
services.description: Open-source advanced enterprise wiki platform for building collaborative web applications, knowledge bases and intranets.
services.installation: Uses the official xwiki image (Tomcat, postgres variant) on internal port 8080. Deploy with the bundled PostgreSQL add-on; the DB_USER/DB_PASSWORD/DB_DATABASE/DB_HOST env vars point XWiki at the '{{KUBERO_APP_NAME}}-postgresql-rw' Postgres service (rotate the Postgres add-on password after install). On first boot XWiki connects to the empty database, runs the Distribution Wizard to install the default flavor (XWiki Standard), and prompts you to create the admin (superadmin) user and password at the app URL, complete the wizard and set a strong admin password before going to production. The permanent directory (config, extensions, attachments cache, Solr search index) lives under /usr/local/xwiki and is persisted by the xwiki-data volume, no external Solr/Elasticsearch is required since XWiki ships an embedded Solr index there. Increase the app pod size (JVM heap) for larger wikis. The 'stable-postgres-tomcat' tag tracks the latest stable XWiki release, switch to an 'lts-postgres-tomcat' tag for long-term-support builds.

services.name: yopass
services.description: Self-hosted web service for securely sharing one-time secrets, passwords and files with client-side encryption and automatic expiry.
services.installation: Yopass stores encrypted secrets ephemerally in Memcached (deployed as the bundled add-on); secrets auto-expire by TTL so no persistent volume is required. The server is reached at the memcached add-on host yopass-memcached:11211 via the MEMCACHED env var. Secrets are encrypted in the browser before upload, so the server never sees plaintext. Set the public URL via the BASE_URL env (already wired to {{KUBERO_APP_URL}}) so generated share links are correct. For production behind the Kubero ingress, optionally set TRUSTED_PROXIES to the ingress CIDR. No database credentials to rotate.

services.name: yourls
services.description: Your Own URL Shortener: a small, self-hosted PHP service to run your own short-link service with stats, plugins, and a developer API.
services.installation: YOURLS only supports MySQL/MariaDB (no PostgreSQL), so this template ships the mariadb add-on. The admin account is bootstrapped from YOURLS_USER / YOURLS_PASS on first boot, and YOURLS_SITE is bound to the deploy URL via {{KUBERO_APP_URL}} (must be the exact public URL, no trailing slash). The admin UI lives at /admin/. Before going to production, rotate the MariaDB password, the YOURLS_PASS admin password, and the YOURLS_COOKIEKEY (openssl rand -hex 32). User-supplied plugins, custom pages, and language packs persist on the mounted volume at /var/www/html/user.

services.name: zammad
services.description: Open-source web-based help desk and customer support / ticketing system with email, chat, social and a knowledge base.
services.installation: Zammad is a MULTI-CONTAINER stack. All Zammad roles share ONE image (ghcr.io/zammad/zammad) and differ only by their entrypoint command. This template runs the PRIMARY web tier, command 'zammad-nginx' (HTTP on port 8080). To make Zammad fully functional you MUST also deploy these companion services from the SAME ghcr.io/zammad/zammad image with these commands, all sharing the same env vars and the zammad-storage (/opt/zammad/storage) volume: 'zammad-init' (run-once DB migrate/seed; must finish before the others come up), 'zammad-railsserver' (Rails app/API, internal :3000 that nginx proxies to), 'zammad-websocket' (websocket/ActionCable), 'zammad-scheduler' (background jobs / delayed jobs). An optional 'zammad-backup' container handles dumps. Bundled add-ons here cover the datastores: postgres (primary DB), memcached (Rails cache), and valkey (Redis-compatible, used for the websocket session store and job queue). Full-text search needs Elasticsearch, which is NOT one of the available operator add-ons and is omitted: deploy an Elasticsearch instance separately (image elasticsearch:9, single-node, xpack.security.enabled=false, vm.max_map_count=262144) and set ELASTICSEARCH_ENABLED=true / ELASTICSEARCH_HOST / ELASTICSEARCH_PORT to enable search; Zammad boots and works without it (search disabled). Rotate POSTGRES_PASS (default 'zammad') before exposing publicly. Set ZAMMAD_FQDN to your real hostname. On first load the web UI runs the getting-started wizard to create the first admin/organization; configure inbound/outbound email channels there afterwards.
